<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729385 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00011: Activity simulation engine

- page_id: `136729385`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729385/Activity+simulation+engine
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:10.604+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Activity simulation
- labels: ['activity-simulation-engine']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

129494513

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

129494516

INLINE

1264288996

INLINE

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

129494514

INLINE

Cameo Simulation Toolkit provides an Activity simulation engine that allows you to run an Activity Simulation on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x'] or Activity Elements. Cameo Simulation Toolkit also includes the implementation of OMG Semantics of a Foundational Subset for Executable UML Models (fUML), an executable subset of standard UML, that can be used to define the structural and Behavioral semantics of systems. fUML defines a basic virtual machine for the Unified Modeling Language and supports specific abstractions enabling compliant models to be transformed into various executable forms for verification, integration, and deployment.

Various UML Activity diagram concepts are supported, including Object and Control Flows, Behavior and Operation Calls, sending Signals via Connectors with or without Ports in Internal Structure, accepting Signals and Time Events, Pins, Parameters, Decisions, Structured Activity Nodes, and many more.

The Activity simulation engine features include the following

- fUML 1.3 specification support.
- Any Action languages in opaqueBehaviors, opaqueExpressions, Decisions, Guards, and Constraints (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Integration with MATLAB' space='CST2024x'] for more details).
- CallBehaviorAction with nested diagrams simulation and animation.
- SendSignalAction to send a Signal to a global Event queue to be used by the system level but not by the subsystem, e.g., in State Machine. NoteSendSignalAction has the **Target** input Pin for specifying the signal receiver. If this Pin is omitted, the signal instance will be sent to the context itself.The **On Port** property on SendSignalAction is used for sending signals via the specified port.
- CallOperationAction through a Port.
- Sending Signals through a Port.
- Support for Decision Nodes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Probability' space='CST2024x'] over all outgoing edges.
- Support for Decision Nodes with a Decision input that provides input to Guard specifications on outgoing edges from each Decision Node.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The supported Decision input for Decision nodes.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- You can simulate only Activities that are owned by a Package or a Class. As a workaround, the CallBehavior Actions, owned by the Call Behaviors in a Package, will be used for the entry/do/exit Behaviors in States.

- The Guards on an ObjectFlow are not Boolean expressions in fUML. They should contain a value that matches the runtime value that flows on the ObjectFlow during simulation.

To change to a regular UML (Boolean expression)

1. On the main menu, click Options > Project and select Simulation on the left of the Project Options dialog.
2. Select the Use fUML Decision Semantics value check box so that the value becomes false. The value is false by default in the UML mode.

- A decision value can be passed as a parameter of Behavior of an opaque expression through a pin of Action. If a valid return value is from an assigned Behavior of a Guard, the body of the Guard will be ignored. However, if the Behavior is <unspecified>, the body of the Guard will be used.

Most of the elements on an Activity diagram are supported as follows

- Control Flow
- Object Flow (Transformation is not supported)
- Input Pin (Illegal type is not supported)
- Output Pin
- Activity Final Node
- Flow Final Node
- Activity Parameter Node: The parameter of the Activity Parameter Node must be defined. If the Activity Parameter Node violates the rule, Simulation will print a warning message in the **Console** pane .
- Decision Node
- Merge Node
- Join Node
- Fork Node
- Structured Activity Node
- Conditional Node
- Loop Node: the setup part will not be executed as the fUML specification. The number of Input Pins of the Loop Node must be equal to Output Pins. If the Loop Node violates the rule, Simulation will print an error message in the **Console** pane .
- Expansion Region
- Expansion Node
- Object Node
- Central Buffer Node
- Data Store Node
- Actions
- AcceptEventAction
- AddStructuralFeatureValueAction
- CallBehaviorAction
- CallOperationAction
- ClearAssociationAction
- ClearStructuralFeatureAction
- CreateLinkAction
- CreateObjectAction
- DestroyLinkAction
- DestroyObjectAction
- OpaqueAction
- ReadExtentAction
- ReadIsClassifiedObjectAction
- ReadLinkAction
- ReadSelfAction
- ReadStructuralFeatureAction
- ReclassifyObjectAction
- ReduceAction
- RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction
- SendSignalAction
- StartObjectBehaviorAction
- TestIdentityAction
- ValueSpecificationAction

##### fUML engine settings

You can customize Behaviors of the fUML engine for Simulation to match your project Requirements through the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='CST2024x']** dialog as shown below.

To customize the fUML engine settings in the **Project options** dialog

1. On the main menu, click Options and select Project . The Project options dialog opens.
2. On the left pane, click General > Simulation .
3. Go to the fUML Engine group and set any of the properties as desired. [ATTACHMENT filename='settings_for_fUML_engine.png']

The following table describes the project options of the fUML engine.

| Property | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Use fUML Decision semantics | If set to true (false by default when in the UML mode), all Guards will be solved to true when the flowing token value matches the Guard value (instead of evaluating every Guard as Boolean operation). |
| Auto Create fUML Object of Output | If set to true, automatically create an fUML object of output. |
| Pass Caller Context | If set to true, pass the caller context to the called Behavior if it does not have its own context specified. Otherwise, use the Behavior itself as context. |
| Terminate Nested Behaviors | If set to true, terminate nested Part Behaviors if their parent object Behavior is terminated. |
| Terminate Streaming Behaviors by Output Parameter Multiplicity | If set to true, a streaming Activity terminates when each of its output parameters receives a cumulative number of values equal to the upper bound of the parameter multiplicity. If set to false, a streaming Activity terminates only when forced by the Activity final node or termination of the Activity that invoked it. |
| Allow Concurrent Allocated Activities | If set to true, Activities can be executed in parallel even if the allocated resource is busy. If set to false, only one allocated Activity is executed for an object represented in an Activity Partition.If the Allow Concurrent Alllocated Activities option is set to true, it is recommended to use a time event to control the execution of parallel activities. A time event will increase the simulation performance in case one parallelly executed activity needs to get a response from another in order to start. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can instantiate a nested composite structure using the standard PSCS construction mechanism by default.

##### ReadLine support

ReadLine is a function that allows the user to enter value through the input line on the **Console** pane. A Call Behavior Action can be set Behavior as **ReadLine [fUML_Library::BasicInputOutput]** using **fUML_Library.mdzip** from the **Use Project** dialog. Before using the ReadLine function, you need to include **fUML_Library.mdzip** in the project first.

To open the **Use Project** dialog and include **fUML_Library.mdzip**

1. Click File > Use Project > Use Local Project from the main menu to open the Use Project dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. In the **Select a project to use** area, select the **From predefined location** option.

3. In the **Paths to used projects** list, select **<install.root>\modelLibraries**.

4. In the directory tree list, select **fUML-Library** and click **Next**> to proceed to the next step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

5. You will be at the **Specify usage options** step. Click **Finish**. The **Question** dialog opens to ask you about showing auxiliary resources in the Containment tree. Click **Yes**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Then a Call Behavior Action can now be set Behavior as a ReadLine Element. The ReadLine Element will be shown with two default Pins, i.e., result and errorStatus. During the simulation, the ReadLine Element is executed to allow entering value through the input line on the **Console** pane. The result of the ReadLine Element can be used by other Elements with any proper data types, e.g., Guard, as in the following figure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### ReadLine support allows entering value through the input line on the Console pane.

##### Guards in Swimlanes

Simulation supports Guards in Swimlanes in the Activity diagram as shown in the figure below. See also [Using Guards on Transitions](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Using+Guards+on+Transitions) and [Swimlane](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024x/Swimlane).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Guards in Swimlanes are supported in the Activity diagram.

129494512

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Decision and Merge' space='MD2024x']
- Behavior
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d9ffa123-5497-42a0-8012-f986098b62ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">129494513</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2e5948b-80bc-4990-b4b7-c61bcf9bcd00"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">129494516</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03f54a54-8d56-4f86-b523-bb615bb0f42f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1264288996</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a464f4ab-723d-4ce2-a4ca-c65fba6e632d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69a2cc27-b546-405c-871d-9bbc77f998d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">129494514</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Cameo Simulation Toolkit provides an Activity simulation engine that allows you to run an Activity Simulation on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or Activity Elements. Cameo Simulation Toolkit also includes the implementation of OMG Semantics of a Foundational Subset for Executable UML Models (fUML), an executable subset of standard UML, that can be used to define the structural and Behavioral semantics of systems. fUML defines a basic virtual machine for the Unified Modeling Language and supports specific abstractions enabling compliant models to be transformed into various executable forms for verification, integration, and deployment. </p><p>Various UML Activity diagram concepts are supported, including Object and Control Flows, Behavior and Operation Calls, sending Signals via Connectors with or without Ports in Internal Structure, accepting Signals and Time Events, Pins, Parameters, Decisions, Structured Activity Nodes, and many more. </p><p>The Activity simulation engine features include the following</p><ul><li>fUML 1.3 specification support.</li><li><p>Any Action languages in opaqueBehaviors, opaqueExpressions, Decisions, Guards, and Constraints (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Integration with MATLAB" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Integration with MATLAB®]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more details).</p></li><li>CallBehaviorAction with nested diagrams simulation and animation.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">SendSignalAction to send a Signal to a global Event queue to be used by the system level but not by the subsystem, e.g., in State Machine.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52577d0a-89c7-4766-b427-f3ba5026daca"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>SendSignalAction has the <strong>Target</strong> input Pin for specifying the signal receiver. If this Pin is omitted, the signal instance will be sent to the context itself.</li><li>The <strong>On Port</strong> property on SendSignalAction is used for sending signals via the specified port.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>CallOperationAction through a Port.</li><li>Sending Signals through a Port.</li><li>Support for Decision Nodes with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Probability" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[probabilities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> over all outgoing edges.</li><li>Support for Decision Nodes with a Decision input that provides input to Guard specifications on outgoing edges from each Decision Node. </li></ul><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Supported Decision Input forDecision Nodes" ac:alt="Supported Decision Input forDecision Nodes"><ri:attachment ri:filename="decision_input.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity simulation engine" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The supported Decision input for Decision nodes.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5a84a3f-fede-4c4d-8fe6-fcf63f7af077"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can simulate only Activities that are owned by a Package or a Class. As a workaround, the CallBehavior Actions, owned by the Call Behaviors in a Package, will be used for the entry/do/exit Behaviors in States.</li></ul><ul><li>The Guards on an ObjectFlow are not Boolean expressions in fUML. They should contain a value that matches the runtime value that flows on the ObjectFlow during simulation.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To change to a regular UML (Boolean expression)</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong> and select <strong>Simulation</strong> on the left of the <strong>Project </strong><strong>Options</strong> dialog.</li><li>Select the <strong>Use fUML Decision</strong><strong>Semantics value</strong> check box so that the value becomes false. The value is false by default in the UML mode.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ul><li>A decision value can be passed as a parameter of Behavior of an opaque expression through a pin of Action. If a valid return value is from an assigned Behavior of a Guard, the body of the Guard will be ignored. However, if the Behavior is &lt;unspecified&gt;, the body of the Guard will be used.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Most of the elements on an Activity diagram are supported as follows</p><ul><li>Control Flow</li><li>Object Flow (Transformation is not supported)</li><li>Input Pin (Illegal type is not supported)</li><li>Output Pin</li><li>Activity Final Node</li><li>Flow Final Node</li><li>Activity Parameter Node: The parameter of the Activity Parameter Node must be defined. If the Activity Parameter Node violates the rule, Simulation will print a warning message in the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Console+pane"><strong>Console</strong> pane</a>.</li><li>Decision Node</li><li>Merge Node</li><li>Join Node</li><li>Fork Node</li><li>Structured Activity Node</li><li>Conditional Node</li><li>Loop Node: the setup part will not be executed as the fUML specification. The number of Input Pins of the Loop Node must be equal to Output Pins. If the Loop Node violates the rule, Simulation will print an error message in the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Console+pane"><strong>Console</strong> pane</a>.</li><li>Expansion Region</li><li>Expansion Node</li><li>Object Node  </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Central Buffer Node </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Data Store Node </li><li>Actions</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">AcceptEventAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">AddStructuralFeatureValueAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">CallBehaviorAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">CallOperationAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ClearAssociationAction </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ClearStructuralFeatureAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">CreateLinkAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">CreateObjectAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">DestroyLinkAction </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">DestroyObjectAction </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">OpaqueAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ReadExtentAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ReadIsClassifiedObjectAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ReadLinkAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ReadSelfAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ReadStructuralFeatureAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ReclassifyObjectAction </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ReduceAction </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">SendSignalAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">StartObjectBehaviorAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">TestIdentityAction</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">ValueSpecificationAction </li></ul><h3><br />fUML engine settings</h3><p>You can customize Behaviors of the fUML engine for Simulation to match your project Requirements through the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog as shown below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize the fUML engine settings in the <strong><span>Project options</span></strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> and select <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong><span>Project options</span></strong> dialog opens.</li><li>On the left pane, click <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Simulation</strong>.</li><li>Go to the <strong>fUML Engine</strong> group and set any of the properties as desired.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="settings_for_fUML_engine.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity simulation engine" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The following table describes the project options of the fUML engine.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 25.1851%;" /><col style="width: 74.8295%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Use fUML Decision semantics</strong></td><td colspan="1">If set to <em>true</em> (false by default when in the UML mode), all Guards will be solved to true when the flowing token value matches the Guard value (instead of evaluating every Guard as Boolean operation).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Auto Create fUML Object of Output</strong></td><td colspan="1">If set to <em>true</em>, automatically create an fUML object of output.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Pass Caller Context</strong></td><td>If set to <em>true</em>, pass the caller context to the called Behavior if it does not have its own context specified. Otherwise, use the Behavior itself as context.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Terminate Nested Behaviors</strong></td><td>If set to <em>true</em>, terminate nested Part Behaviors if their parent object Behavior is terminated.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Terminate Streaming Behaviors by Output Parameter Multiplicity</strong></td><td colspan="1">If set to <em>true</em>, a streaming Activity terminates when each of its output parameters receives a cumulative number of values equal to the upper bound of the parameter multiplicity. If set to <em>false</em>, a streaming Activity terminates only when forced by the Activity final node or termination of the Activity that invoked it.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Allow Concurrent Allocated Activities</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If set to </span><em>true</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, Activities can be executed in parallel even if the allocated resource is busy. If set to </span><em>false</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, only one allocated Activity is executed for an object represented in an Activity Partition.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36b49eeb-7168-4820-a385-1b5c84dda635"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">If the <strong>Allow Concurrent Alllocated Activities</strong> option is set to <em>true</em>, it is recommended to use a time event to control the execution of parallel activities. A time event will increase the simulation performance in case one parallelly executed activity needs to get a response from another in order to start.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b74d3fce-380f-433e-a4f9-3ee140a78c00"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can instantiate a nested composite structure using the standard PSCS construction mechanism by default.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />ReadLine support</h3><p>ReadLine is a function that allows the user to enter value through the input line on the <strong>Console</strong> pane. A Call Behavior Action can be set Behavior as <strong>ReadLine [fUML_Library::BasicInputOutput]</strong> using <strong>fUML_Library.mdzip</strong> from the <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog. Before using the ReadLine function, you need to include <strong>fUML_Library.mdzip</strong> in the project first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog and include <strong>fUML_Library.mdzip</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Project</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Local Project</strong> from the main menu to open the <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fUML Libary Including.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity simulation engine" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. In the <strong>Select a project to use</strong> area, select the <strong>From predefined location</strong> option.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. In the <strong>Paths to used projects</strong> list, select <strong>&lt;install.root&gt;\modelLibraries</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">4. In the directory tree list, select <strong>fUML-Library</strong> and click <strong>Next</strong>&gt; to proceed to the next step.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fUML Library.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity simulation engine" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">5. You will be at the <strong>Specify usage options</strong> step. Click <strong>Finish</strong>. The <strong>Question</strong> dialog opens to ask you about showing auxiliary resources in the Containment tree. Click <strong>Yes</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fUML Library Question Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity simulation engine" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="inline-comment-marker"><br /></p><p class="inline-comment-marker">Then a Call Behavior Action can now be set Behavior as a ReadLine Element. The ReadLine Element will be shown with two default Pins, i.e., result and errorStatus. During the simulation, the ReadLine Element is executed to allow entering value through the input line on the <strong>Console</strong> pane. The result of the ReadLine Element can be used by other Elements with any proper data types, e.g., Guard, as in the following figure</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="7572 Readline Support.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity simulation engine" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">ReadLine support allows entering value through the input line on the Console pane.</h6><h3><br />Guards in Swimlanes</h3><p>Simulation supports Guards in Swimlanes in the Activity diagram as shown in the figure below. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Using+Guards+on+Transitions">Using Guards on Transitions</a> and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024x/Swimlane">Swimlane</a>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="9906-1.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity simulation engine" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Guards in Swimlanes are supported in the Activity diagram.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c69330f-e533-4a29-b90b-7e9e7f5f1bf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">129494512</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Decision and Merge" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Behavior">Behavior</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729326 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00012: Adapting models for State Machine simulation

- page_id: `136729326`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729326/Adapting+models+for+State+Machine+simulation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:08.649+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > State Machine simulation
- labels: ['adapting-model-for-state-machine-simulation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

116136583

116136585

116136584

Currently, Cameo Simulation Toolkit can simulate only those elements whose types are specified in the supported elements table in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Supported elements' space='CST2024x']. Thus, you must modify your model so that only the supported (executable) elements are included in your State Machine diagram.

State Machine simulation can be adapted in several different ways, as outlined below:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining Triggers on Transitions' space='CST2024x']Defining Triggers on Transitions Changing the States of runtime objects on Transitions by defining Triggers on Transitions.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Guards on Transitions' space='CST2024x'] Specifying Guard conditions on Transitions by using any action languages.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State' space='CST2024x']Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State Defining Behaviors that can be an Activity, a State Machine, an Interaction, or an OpaqueBehavior of States for Activities.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Internal Transition' space='CST2024x'] 
Creating an Internal Transition to change the behavior without changing the overall state.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Signal properties mapping to Behavior parameters' space='CST2024x']Signal properties mapping to Behavior parameters Mapping Signal properties to Behavior parameters by sending Signals to target objects to carry values to input parameters.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State activation semantics' space='CST2024x']State activation semantics Specifying State Activation semantics regarding when Cameo Simulation Toolkit activates Entry Behaviors on activating entries of States.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Completion Events and Transitions' space='CST2024x']Completion Events and Transitions Enabling or disabling completion of Events and Transitions during State Machine simulation.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deferred Events' space='CST2024x']Deferred Events Utilizing deferrable Triggers to save deferred Events for future processing until States are entered.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b10c121c-b74e-4610-adcd-30c44833ab8d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">116136583</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02408404-d1f0-4e44-8dfe-fc86a9f9453b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">116136585</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="006157c4-5082-4940-b8d4-39938c143ac7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">116136584</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Currently, Cameo Simulation Toolkit can simulate only those elements whose types are specified in the supported elements table in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Supported elements" /></ac:link>. Thus, you must modify your model so that only the supported (executable) elements are included in your State Machine diagram. </p><p><br /></p><p>State Machine simulation can be adapted in several different ways, as outlined below:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Defining Triggers on Transitions" /><ac:link-body>Defining Triggers on Transitions<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Changing the States of runtime objects on Transitions by defining Triggers on Transitions.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Using Guards on Transitions" /></ac:link><br />Specifying Guard conditions on Transitions by using any action languages.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State" /><ac:link-body>Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Defining Behaviors that can be an Activity, a State Machine, an Interaction, or an OpaqueBehavior of States for Activities.<br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Internal Transition" /></ac:link><br />Creating an Internal Transition to change the behavior without changing the overall state.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br /></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Signal properties mapping to Behavior parameters" /><ac:link-body>Signal properties mapping to Behavior parameters<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Mapping Signal properties to Behavior parameters by sending Signals to target objects to carry values to input parameters.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="State activation semantics" /><ac:link-body>State activation semantics<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Specifying State Activation semantics regarding when Cameo Simulation Toolkit activates Entry Behaviors on activating entries of States.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Completion Events and Transitions" /><ac:link-body>Completion Events and Transitions<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Enabling or disabling completion of Events and Transitions during State Machine simulation.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Deferred Events" /><ac:link-body>Deferred Events<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Utilizing deferrable Triggers to save deferred Events for future processing until States are entered.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136730098 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00013: Adding a reset Activity to the ready State

- page_id: `136730098`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136730098/Adding+a+reset+Activity+to+the+ready+State
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:46.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Tutorial > Stopwatch model sample > Creating an executable stopwatch model > Adding Activities to the ready and running States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a **reset** Activity to the Entry to the **ready** State

1. Right-click the ready State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram and select Specification to open the State's Specification window.
2. Select the Entry row and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3-dot-btn.png'] button. A context menu will open.
3. Select Activity . A new Activity will be created for the entry to the ready State. [ATTACHMENT filename='specification-of-state-ready.png']
4. Name the created Activity "reset" .
5. Click Close to close the Specification window. The reset Activity will be added to the entry to the ready State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram as shown . [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav0f7253037c7049b9c8ab0aaa0450c45a.png']
6. Add an Activity diagram to the created reset Activity of the ready State by right-clicking the reset Activity in the containment browser and select New Diagram > Activity Diagram . A new Activity diagram will be created. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav52e8e6b88aa2892122cd86fc1e4225a3.png']
7. Use the default name of the diagram, which is "reset".
8. Add a CallBehaviorAction to the resetTime Activity on the reset Activity diagram by dragging the resetTime Activity from the containment browser to the reset Activity Diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav359c7ae7ada3a14e838088b1255904ba.png']
9. Complete the reset Activity by adding an Initial stage and a Final Activity stage to the reset Activity diagram and connect the two stages and the resetTime Action using Control Flow , which is on the Activity Diagram toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddavfd0f163c0d3b56bd0fce375d9b07eccf.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A context menu will open.</li><li>Select <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Activity</strong>. A new Activity will be created for the entry to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">ready</strong> State. <br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Adding an Activity to the Entry of the Ready State" ac:alt="Adding an Activity to the Entry of the Ready State"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification-of-state-ready.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Adding a reset Activity to the ready State" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Name the created Activity <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">&quot;reset&quot;</em>.</li><li>Click <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Close</strong> to close the Specification window. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136730106 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00015: Adding an increase activity to the running state

- page_id: `136730106`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136730106/Adding+an+increase+activity+to+the+running+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:46.532+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Tutorial > Stopwatch model sample > Creating an executable stopwatch model > Adding Activities to the ready and running States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Next, we are going to create an increased Activity, which will be called when the running State is reached. To add the Activity to the running State, follow the same steps used to create the **reset** Activity for the entry to the **ready** State.

To add an Increase Activity to the entry to the running State

1. Right-click the running State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram and select Specification to open the State's Specification window.
2. Select the Entry row and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3-dot-btn.png'] button. A context menu will open. Select Activity to add a new Activity to the Entry to the running State.
3. Name the created Activity "increase" .
4. Click Close to close the Specification window. The increase Activity will be added to the entry to the running State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram as shown in the following figure [ATTACHMENT filename='worddave0a06c4dc481ca850e9ec15da65c084f.png']
5. Add an Activity diagram to the created increase Activity and use the default name, which is "increase".
6. Add a CallBehaviorAction to the increaseTime Activity on the increase Activity diagram by dragging the increaseTime Activity from the containment browser to the increase Activity diagram.
7. Add an Initial stage and a Final Activity stage to the increase Activity diagram and connect the two stages and the increaseTime action with Control Flow , which is on the Activity Diagram toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddavbceebb6243daff43453f3f6f09db8037.png']

At this point, the StopWatch has been completely modeled. You can now execute the StopWatch class using Cameo Simulation Toolkit.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Next, we are going to create an increased Activity, which will be called when the running State is reached. To add the Activity to the running State, follow the same steps used to create the <strong>reset</strong> Activity for the entry to the <strong>ready</strong> State. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>To add an Increase Activity to the entry to the running State</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the <strong>running</strong> State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram and select <strong>Specification</strong> to open the State's Specification window.</li><li>Select the Entry row and click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3-dot-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Adding an increase activity to the running state" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. A context menu will open. Select <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Activity</strong> to add a new Activity to the Entry to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">running</strong> State.</li><li>Name the created Activity <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">&quot;increase&quot;</em>.</li><li>Click <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Close</strong> to close the Specification window. The increase Activity will be added to the entry to the running State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram as shown in the following figure<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Adding the Increase Activity to the Entry of the Running State" ac:alt="Adding the Increase Activity to the Entry of the Running State"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddave0a06c4dc481ca850e9ec15da65c084f.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Adding an increase activity to the running state" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Add an Activity diagram to the created <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increase</strong> Activity and use the default name, which is &quot;increase&quot;.</li><li>Add a CallBehaviorAction to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increaseTime</strong> Activity on the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increase</strong> Activity diagram by dragging the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increaseTime</strong> Activity from the containment browser to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increase</strong> Activity diagram.</li><li>Add an Initial stage and a Final Activity stage to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increase</strong> Activity diagram and connect the two stages and the increaseTime action with <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Control</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Flow</strong>, which is on the Activity Diagram toolbar. <br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="A Complete Model of the Increase Activity" ac:alt="A Complete Model of the Increase Activity"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavbceebb6243daff43453f3f6f09db8037.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Adding an increase activity to the running state" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />At this point, the StopWatch has been completely modeled. You can now execute the StopWatch class using Cameo Simulation Toolkit.</p>
````

## PAGE 00016: Adding breakpoints

- page_id: `136729302`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729302/Adding+breakpoints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:06.094+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation debugging > Breakpoints
- labels: ['adding-breakpoints']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add a Breakpoint to a model element using the model's **Add** **Breakpoint(s)** context menu.

To add a Breakpoint to a model element

- Right-click a model element either in the containment browser or on a symbol of the model element on the diagram and select Simulation > Add Breakpoint(s) .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729804 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00017: Adjunct property

- page_id: `136729804`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729804/Adjunct+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:31.785+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['adjunct-property']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Adjunct Property is a property to which the stereotype «AdjunctProperty» is applied. Its tag definition **principal** **: Element[1]** is for an element to determine the value of an adjunct property. With regard to the SysML Specification, a principal can be a Connector, a CallAction, an ObjectNode, a Variable, a Parameter, a Submachine State, or an InteractionUse. Now Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the adjunct property whose principal is a CallBehaviorAction, a CallOperationAction, an ActivityParameterNode, or a Parameter.

If you use an adjunct property to represent an entry/do/exit behavior of a state, an effect behavior of a transition, and a method behavior of a call operation in a CallMessage in MagicDraw SysML plugin and Cameo System Modeler, you can use Cameo Simulation Toolkit to show you the value of such adjunct property during simulation.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**•** CallBehaviorAction and CallOperationAction are subtypes of CallAction. 
**•** ActivityParameterNode is a subtype of ObjectNode.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Executing an object with Adjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties.

The preceding shows an adjunct property in the **Integrator** activity. The adjunct property is typed by the activity **TimeDifferent**. Besides having the Integrator activity as a classifier behavior, the block **Integrator** also contains a classifier behavior property typed by the Integrator activity (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Classifier Behavior property' space='CST2024x'] for more information about the classifier behavior property). When the Integrator object is initialized from the block **Integrator**, the behavior simulation of its classifier behavior will start and be set as a value of the classifier behavior property. While executing the classifier behavior, Cameo Simulation Toolkit will activate the call behavior action **getTimeDiff**, which is the principal of the adjunct property. It will create the behavior simulation of the **getTimeDiff** and set it as the value of the adjunct property in the **Variables** pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Adjunct Property is a property to which the stereotype «AdjunctProperty» is applied. Its tag definition <strong>principal</strong> <strong>: Element[1]</strong> is for an element to determine the value of an adjunct property. With regard to the SysML Specification, a principal can be a Connector, a CallAction, an ObjectNode, a Variable, a Parameter, a Submachine State, or an InteractionUse. Now Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the adjunct property whose principal is a CallBehaviorAction, a CallOperationAction, an ActivityParameterNode, or a Parameter. </p><p>If you use an adjunct property to represent an entry/do/exit behavior of a state, an effect behavior of a transition, and a method behavior of a call operation in a CallMessage in MagicDraw SysML plugin and Cameo System Modeler, you can use Cameo Simulation Toolkit to show you the value of such adjunct property during simulation. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cee7410-4acb-4e4c-8765-ac3cb789e79f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"><strong>•</strong></span> CallBehaviorAction and CallOperationAction are subtypes of CallAction.<br /><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"><strong>•</strong></span> ActivityParameterNode is a subtype of ObjectNode. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Executing an Object withAdjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties" ac:alt="Executing an Object withAdjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties"><ri:attachment ri:filename="execute_objectwithAdjunct.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Adjunct property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Executing an object with Adjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties.</h6><p>The preceding shows an adjunct property in the <strong>Integrator</strong> activity. The adjunct property is typed by the activity <strong>TimeDifferent</strong>. Besides having the Integrator activity as a classifier behavior, the block <strong>Integrator</strong> also contains a classifier behavior property typed by the Integrator activity (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Classifier Behavior property" /></ac:link> for more information about the classifier behavior property). When the Integrator object is initialized from the block <strong>Integrator</strong>, the behavior simulation of its classifier behavior will start and be set as a value of the classifier behavior property. While executing the classifier behavior, Cameo Simulation Toolkit will activate the call behavior action <strong>getTimeDiff</strong>, which is the principal of the adjunct property. It will create the behavior simulation of the <strong>getTimeDiff</strong> and set it as the value of the adjunct property in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane. </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729102 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00019: Animation

- page_id: `136729102`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729102/Animation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:58:50.919+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide
- labels: ['simulation-animation', 'animation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1440884490

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1440884492

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1440884491

Active elements on a diagram will be annotated during simulation using the same annotation mechanism used in the active validation:

- Active and visited elements will be annotated in red and green respectively.
- Runtime values will be visible in the tooltip of annotated elements.
- Values flown through pins of action will be visible in the tooltip of annotated actions.
- Duration of called Activities will be visible in the tooltip of annotated call Behavior Actions if you simulate the Activity with a specific duration mode.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you select **true** as the value of the **silent** tag in the Simulation Configurations, Cameo Simulation Toolkit will not animate simulation and will simulate models in silent mode (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation Configuration and UI modeling' space='CST2024x'] for more information).

1440884489

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729113 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00020: Animation speed option

- page_id: `136729113`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729113/Animation+speed+option
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:58:51.820+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Animation
- labels: ['animation-speed']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Animation Speed** in the Simulation Configuration is the speed to run a particular Configuration animation. The default value of Animation Speed is **95%**.****You can adjust it to avoid problems with delay when the default speed is slow and large numbers of model elements are running. To run your animation at a pace that is faster or slower than the default animation speed, change the value of the **Animation** **Speed** option to your preferred one in the Specification window of the *<<SimulationConfig>>*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Animation Speed option in <<SimulationConfig>> is specified at the default value of 95%.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**Animation Speed** will not affect animation in the Parametric diagram since no animation is delayed during parametric solving.

The **Animation Speed** setting is visible through diagram animation. For example, if the speed is specified at the value of 50%, the animation must be slower than the default value 95%. The speed setting is consistent with the Simulation speed bar control; e.g., if the animation speed is specified at 50%, the speed bar is set to the middle as shown in the following figure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The animation speed selected is consistent with the Animation speed bar.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The speed of the animation does not affect the animation in the Parametric diagram as no animation is delayed during parametric solving.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="Standard"><strong>Animation Speed</strong> in the Simulation Configuration is the speed to run a particular Configuration animation. The default value of Animation Speed is <strong>95%</strong>.<strong> </strong>You can adjust it to avoid problems with delay when the default speed is slow and large numbers of model elements are running. To run your animation at a pace that is faster or slower than the default animation speed, change the value of the <strong>Animation</strong> <strong>Speed</strong> option to your preferred one in the Specification window of the <em>&lt;&lt;SimulationConfig&gt;&gt;</em>.</p><p class="Standard"> </p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The default animation speed in the Simulation Config is 95." ac:alt="The default animation speed in the Simulation Config is 95."><ri:attachment ri:filename="animation_speed.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Animation speed option" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Animation Speed option in &lt;&lt;SimulationConfig&gt;&gt; is specified at the default value of 95%.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba65e88c-9124-4081-b7fa-c5732540244c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Animation Speed</strong> will not affect animation in the Parametric diagram since no animation is delayed during parametric solving.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="Textbody">The <strong>Animation Speed</strong> setting is visible through diagram animation. For example, if the speed is specified at the value of 50%, the animation must be slower than the default value 95%. The speed setting is consistent with the Simulation speed bar control; e.g., if the animation speed is specified at 50%, the speed bar is set to the middle as shown in the following figure </p><p class="Standard"> </p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The animation speed is consistent with the animation speed bar." ac:alt="The animation speed is consistent with the animation speed bar."><ri:attachment ri:filename="anim_speedbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Animation speed option" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The animation speed selected is consistent with the Animation speed bar.</h6><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69901d8a-8dae-4595-a42b-df8d70ed1eb4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The speed of the animation does not affect the animation in the Parametric diagram as no animation is delayed during parametric solving.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=142934180 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00021: API Changes

- page_id: `142934180`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/142934180/API+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-11-09T10:23:06.165+01:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Developer Guide > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table lists the old and new values of the constants for SimulationProfile, SimulationOptions, and SysMLConstants.

| Old values up to 2022x Refresh2 | New values from 2024x |
| --- | --- |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.SimulationOptionConstants were removed altogether, instead, use com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions |  |
| SimulationOptionConstants.ACTIVE_COLOR_NAME | SimulationOptions.ACTIVE_COLOR |
| SimulationOptionConstants.ADD_CONTROL_PANEL_NAME | SimulationOptions.ADD_CONTROL_PANEL |
| SimulationOptionConstants.ANIMATION_SPEED_NAME | SimulationOptions.ANIMATION_SPEED |
| SimulationOptionConstants.AUTOSTART_ACTIVE_OPTIONS_NAME | SimulationOptions.AUTO_START_ACTIVE_OPTIONS |
| SimulationOptionConstants.AUTO_CREATE_FUML_OBJECT_OF_OUTPUT_PIN_NAME | SimulationOptions.AUTO_CREATE_FUML_OBJECT_OF_OUTPUT_PIN |
| SimulationOptionConstants.AUTO_OPEN_DIAGRAMS_NAME | SimulationOptions.AUTO_OPEN_DIAGRAMS |
| SimulationOptionConstants.AUTO_START_NAME | SimulationOptions.AUTO_START |
| SimulationOptionConstants.BREAKPOINT_COLOR_NAME | SimulationOptions.BREAKPOINT_COLOR |
| SimulationOptionConstants.CHECK_MODEL_BEFORE_EXECUTION | SimulationOptions.CHECK_MODEL_BEFORE_EXECUTION |
| SimulationOptionConstants.CLONE_REFERENCES_NAME | SimulationOptions.CLONE_REFERENCES |
| SimulationOptionConstants.CONSTRAINT_FAILURE_AS_BREAKPOINT_NAME | SimulationOptions.CONSTRAINT_FAILURE_AS_BREAKPOINT |
| SimulationOptionConstants.DECIMAL_PLACES_NAME | SimulationOptions.DECIMAL_PLACES |
| SimulationOptionConstants.DEFAULT_LANGUAGE_NAME | SimulationOptions.DEFAULT_LANGUAGE |
| SimulationOptionConstants.DEFAULT_PARAMETRIC_EVALUATOR_NAME | SimulationOptions.DEFAULT_PARAMETRIC_EVALUATOR |
| SimulationOptionConstants.DURATION_SIMULATION_MODE_NAME | SimulationOptions.DURATION_SIMULATION_MODE |
| SimulationOptionConstants.ENDTIME_NAME | SimulationOptions.ENDTIME |
| SimulationOptionConstants.ENGINES_PRIORITY_NAME | SimulationOptions.ENGINES_PRIORITY |
| SimulationOptionConstants.EXCLUDED_ELEMENTS_NAME | SimulationOptions.EXCLUDED_ELEMENTS |
| SimulationOptionConstants.EXECUTION_LISTENERS_NAME | SimulationOptions.EXECUTION_LISTENERS |
| SimulationOptionConstants.EXECUTION_TARGET_NAME | SimulationOptions.EXECUTION_TARGET |
| SimulationOptionConstants.EXTERNAL_LIBRARIES_NAME | SimulationOptions.EXTERNAL_LIBRARIES |
| SimulationOptionConstants.EXTERNAL_SOLVER_TIMEOUT_NAME | SimulationOptions.EXTERNAL_SOLVER_TIMEOUT |
| SimulationOptionConstants.FIRE_VALUE_CHANGE_EVENT_NAME | SimulationOptions.AUTOMATIC_PARAMETRIC_RECALCULATIONThe tag was changed to control only the recalculation of constraints, ensuring that the values are consistent on both sides when there is a binding connector. Set to true for parametric models to be automatically recalculated when there is a change in any structural feature value. Set to false to recalculate parametric models manually. |
| SimulationOptionConstants.INITIALIZE_NUMERICAL_VALUE_NAME | SimulationOptions.INITIALIZE_NUMERICAL_VALUE |
| SimulationOptionConstants.INITIALIZE_REFERENCES_NAME | SimulationOptions.INITIALIZE_REFERENCES |
| SimulationOptionConstants.LASTVISITED_COLOR_NAME | SimulationOptions.LAST_VISITED_COLOR |
| SimulationOptionConstants.LOG_NAME | SimulationOptions.LOG |
| SimulationOptionConstants.NUMBER_OF_RUNS_NAME | SimulationOptions.NUMBER_OF_RUNS |
| SimulationOptionConstants.NUMBER_OF_STEPS_NAME | SimulationOptions.NUMBER_OF_STEPS |
| SimulationOptionConstants.OPEN_SIMULATION_PANE_NAME | SimulationOptions.OPEN_SIMULATION_PANE |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729808 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00022: Association Block

- page_id: `136729808`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729808/Association+Block
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:32.430+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['association-block']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Block stereotype extends Class so it can be applied to any specialization of Class including Association Classes. Association Classes are informally called “Association Blocks.” An Association Block can own properties and connectors just like any other Block. Each instance of an Association Block can link together instances of the end Classifiers of the Association. To refer to the linked objects and values of an instance of an Association Block, it is necessary for the modeler to specify which (participant) properties of the Association Block identify the instances being linked at which end of the Association. The value of a participant property on the instance (link) of the Association Block is the value or object at the end of the link corresponding to this end of the Association.

The following figures depict the use of an Association Block in a model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### System initializes «ConnectorProperty» by an Association Block instance according to "Connector" reference (AB) and «ParticipantProperty» (b2, b1. add. b21. b11) according to "End" reference (b1, b2).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Association Block can provide or add anything such as Behaviors and connectors (a, b) so signals can flow. Parametric and all other constraints (a = b + 5) work automatically.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="Standard">A Block stereotype extends Class so it can be applied to any specialization of Class including Association Classes. Association Classes are informally called “Association Blocks.” An Association Block can own properties and connectors just like any other Block. Each instance of an Association Block can link together instances of the end Classifiers of the Association. To refer to the linked objects and values of an instance of an Association Block, it is necessary for the modeler to specify which (participant) properties of the Association Block identify the instances being linked at which end of the Association. The value of a participant property on the instance (link) of the Association Block is the value or object at the end of the link corresponding to this end of the Association.</p><p class="Standard">The following figures depict the use of an Association Block in a model.</p><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connectorproperties.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Association Block" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">System initializes <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8de74124-7f84-4779-963e-c038f38c8909">«ConnectorProperty»</ac:inline-comment-marker> by an Association Block instance according to &quot;Connector&quot; reference (AB) and «ParticipantProperty» (b2, b1. add. b21. b11) according to &quot;End&quot; reference (b1, b2).</h6><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connectors.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Association Block" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">An Association Block can provide or add anything such as Behaviors and connectors (a, b) so signals can flow. Parametric and all other constraints (a = b + 5) work automatically.</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729011 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00023: Attached files supported

- page_id: `136729011`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729011/Attached+files+supported
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:58:47.084+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['attached-file-support']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

610531277

610531279

610531278

Cameo Simulation Toolkit can access files as «AttachedFile» in MagicDraw using only file names (without paths), if there are references to files by names not found in project directories. The attached files supported are as follows:

- **CSV**: Used by the **LoadCSVFile** tag of «TimeSeriesChart». [IMAGE alt='' src='']An attached file of BouncingResult.csv used by the LoadCSVFile tag of «TimeSeriesChart».
- XLSX (Excel) : Used by Excel Helper APIs.
- M (MATLAB) : Used by the Specification tag of «Constraint».
- FMU (FMI) : Used as a «Block» «fmu» for FMI.
- JAR (external libraries) : Used as external libraries (*.JAR) in the script engine.
- SLX : used by the «url» tag of «SimulinkBlock»
- MOS/MO : used by Dymola.

However, if the referenced file is in both the project directory and in the attached file, the following conditions are applied:

1. If the file is in both the project directory and the attached file, the file in «AttachedFile» will be used, and the following warning message will appear in the Console pane, " Warning: Use <file name>in the attached file instead of the project directory. "
2. If the file is in the project directory but not in the attached file, the file in the project directory will be used without any displayed messages.
3. If the file is not in the project directory but is in the attached file, the file in «AttachedFile» will be used, and the following information message will appear in the Console pane, “ INFO: The attached file <file name> is loaded. ”
4. If the file is not in the project directory and not in the attached file, the following error message will appear in the Console pane, “ ERROR: Cannot find file or attached file with '<file name>'.
5. If the mos/mo file (Dymola) is located in both the working directory and the attached file, the file in the current working directory will be used, and the following warning message will appear in the Console pane, "Warning: Use '<file name>.ext' in the working directory instead of the attached file."

610531276

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects with file attachments' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel Import Plugin Documentation' space='EI']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729024 space=CST2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00024: Auto-generating HTML files

- page_id: `136729024`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729024/Auto-generating+HTML+files
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2024-02-13T12:23:23.531+01:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit
- labels: ['generating-html-files', 'html-files']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1437457028

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1437457031

INLINE

152638543

INLINE

**On this page**

33

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1437457029

INLINE

If the**Start Web Server**property of a Simulation Configuration is set to*true,*the Cameo Simulation Toolkit will automatically generate HTML files from all used UI Configurations. This feature saves a lot of time because you are not required to manually export them to get those HTML files. You can then use UI Mockups instead of local UI Mockups and control your model simulation on a web browser from any remote device.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of a Simulation Configuration with the Start Web Server property set to true to auto-generate HTML files.

When the Simulation Configurationis run, the built-in web server will start, HTML files with a hyperlink to each file in the local project folder will be generated, and a URL with a hyperlink will be shown in the simulation **Console** pane (regardless of the **Options** mode you are using in the **Console** pane).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The web server notification, URL to open web UI, and browser types in the simulation Console pane.

##### Embedded browser

Clicking the URL opens a popup menu containing either an Embedded or Web Browser, where you can view and control the simulation through the Web UI. The Embedded Browser allows you to show any web pages in dock-able embedded windows in fixed places. These windows can be moved and docked, and will appear in the modeling toolwindow. When you open a saved project or re-run the project with the embedded browser option, those windows will automatically be shown in the same locations, States (docked/floating), and sizes. When the simulation is terminated, these windows will be closed automatically. To control the simulation through the web UI, you need to enable the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Control panel for Web UI' space=''] option.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Embedded browser UI with Control Panel in modeling tool.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Web browser UI with Control Panel on Google Chrome.

To run a model simulation on the Web Server

1. Double-click the Simulation Configuration to open its Specification window.
2. Set the Start Web Server property to true.
3. Click the URL in the Console pane to open a web-browser as the UI.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

- To prevent overwriting, HTML files will not be regenerated if they have been generated before.
- The HTML files will be generated after you customize the files (e.g., add a logo or change the text color or size), using the same filename.
- You can delete or rename the existing HTML files if you need them to be regenerated.
- Avoid duplicating the name of a «ui» object. If there are two «ui» objects of the same name (e.g. «frame»), only one of them will be auto-generated.
- If you input an illegal data type through the Web UI (embedded/web browser), a warning dialog will appear to warn you about the data type error (number/boolean). For example, you may see the following: IIIegal value, 'abc' is not a number .

##### Generating HTML files

Depending on the circumstances, you may want to manually generate HTML files for further customization of the web UI Mockups. You can manually generate HTML files through either the UI diagram or HTML supported controls using the **Generate HTML**command. However, if the files already exist, a warning message box will open to prevent you from accidentally overwriting them. All generated HTML files will be written and stored in the project's working directory.

To generate HTML

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the User Interface Modeling Diagram toolbar, click **Generate HTML**. HTML files are generated for all the UI elements shown in the diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the Containment tree, right-click the User Interface Modeling diagram or UI element and select Simulation>Generate HTML. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the Internal Block Definition Diagram, right-click the Widget property and select Simulation > Generate HTML. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. If you get a warning that the file already exists, click Yes , to replace the existing file, or click No to cancel the command. After completing the steps above, you should get a notification that the files were generated successfully.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

Anyone with access to the network the web server is on will be able to access the web link via a web browser.

##### Generating and attaching HTML files to Teamwork Cloud projects

You can easily generate and attach HTML files through the UI Modeling diagram (for only the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Server-side simulation' space='']) when the project is hosted on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud and Services' space='TWCloud2024x'].

To generate and attach HTML

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the User Interface Modeling Diagram toolbar, click **Generate and Attach HTML**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the Containment tree, right-click the User Interface Modeling diagram or UI element and select Simulation>Generate HTML. [ATTACHMENT filename='Generate and Attach HTML_Conatainment tree.png']
2. If you get a warning that the file already exists, click**Yes**to replace the existing file, or click**No**to cancel the command.After completing the steps above, you should get a notification that the files were generated successfully.

Every Frame/Table element will automatically generate HTML files that are zipped and attached under the element with the same name as the UI element name. You should get a notification in the**Console**tab with the list of all the files attached to the project.The structure of the zipped file does not include reusable files of the Simulation, for example “js” folder, and “css” files.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1437457027

INLINE

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation console' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

When you open a saved project or re-run the project with the embedded browser option, those windows will automatically be shown in the same locations, States (docked/floating), and sizes. When the simulation is terminated, these windows will be closed automatically. To control the simulation through the web UI, you need to enable the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Control panel for Web UI" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Control Panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> option.</p><p class="Standard"><br /></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Embedded Browser UI with Control Panel.png" /></ac:image>Embedded browser UI with Control Panel in modeling tool.</h6><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Web browser UI with Control Panel through Google Chrome.png" /></ac:image>Web browser UI with Control Panel on Google Chrome.</h6><p class="Standard">To run a model simulation on the Web Server</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the Simulation <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Configuration</span> to open its Specification window.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Set the </span><strong>Start Web Server</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> property to true.</span></li><li>Click the URL in the <strong>Console</strong> pane to open a web-browser as the UI. <br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fe21a3a-9773-4048-a033-cf9af205604c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To prevent overwriting, HTML files will not be regenerated if they have been generated before.</li><li>The HTML files will be generated after you customize the files (e.g., add a logo or change the text color or size), using the same filename.</li><li>You can delete or rename the existing HTML files if you need them to be regenerated.</li><li>Avoid duplicating the name of a «ui» object. /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate and Attach HTML_Toolbar.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the User Interface Modeling diagram or UI element and select <strong>Simulation&gt;Generate HTML.</strong><strong><br /></strong><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate and Attach HTML_Conatainment tree.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you get a warning that the file already exists, click </span><strong>Yes</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> to replace the existing file, or click </span><strong>No</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> to cancel the command.  <br /></span>After completing the steps above, you should get a notification that the files were generated successfully.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41e2a142-6352-4b90-ae1c-f50f9c58070b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Every Frame/Table element will automatically generate HTML files that are zipped and attached under the element with the same name as the UI element name. <span>You should get a notification in the </span><strong>Console</strong><span> tab with the list of all the files attached to the project. </span></span>The structure of the zipped file does not include reusable files of the Simulation, for example “js” folder, and “css” files.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Console Panel.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aad11d82-570d-48e1-8580-3a3090c3dcca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1437457027</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Simulation console" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729019 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00025: Auto-generating web homepage

- page_id: `136729019`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729019/Auto-generating+web+homepage
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:58:47.577+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit
- labels: ['web-project-homepage', 'generating-web-project-homepage']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With the Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit plugin, you can use projects loaded in the modeling tool through a web browser on any remote device. You will need to activate the web server in the Simulation window first, in order to use this capability.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Start Web Server** button on the Simulation window to start/stop the web server.

Once the web server has been started, a notification and a URL will appear in the Console pane of the Simulation window. The URL launches a web UI mockup rather than a local mockup on a web browser. If you click the URL, a web homepage will open in your default web browser. The homepage can load all of the Simulation Configurations of the projectopened in the modeling tool. You cannot switch between the Simulation Configurationsonce initiated.

To open a web homepage

1. Open any simulation project , e.g., Coffee Machine
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='startstopweb_button.png'] in the Simulation window to start the Web Server.
3. Click the URL to open a web homepage in your default web browser.
4. Clickany given SimulationConfiguration to open its related HTMLs as the UI Mockups.
5. Close the web browser pages to end the simulation. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The web homepage of the Web UI allowing you to open different Simulation Configurations on your browser.

#### NOTE: Note

false

Note

- Projects without Simulation Configurations will not appear on the homepage.
- If the Web UI is used, local UI mockups will not be displayed.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

Opening or closing a project in a modeling tool reloads the list of Simulation Configurations on the web homepage.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
