<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136730132 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00109: Executing the StopWatch using Simulation Configuration

- page_id: `136730132`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136730132/Executing+the+StopWatch+using+Simulation+Configuration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:47.512+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Tutorial > Stopwatch model sample > Executing the stopwatch model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to customize model execution by using an Simulation Configuration. The Simulation Configuration is a stereotype that can be applied to a Class Element. This stereotype contains tag definitions for model execution customization such as enable or disable animation and UI mockups. To be able to use the Simulation Configuration, we need to add the *SimulationProfile.mdzip* file to our project. 
 
 To add the *SimulationProfile.mdzip* file to a project

1. Click File > Use Project on the main menu. The Use Project wizard dialog opens.
2. Select the From predefined location option.
3. Select <install.root>\profiles in Project modules paths , and then select SimulationProfile.mdzip . [ATTACHMENT filename='Use Project Dialog.png']
4. Click Next to go to the second step, Module Settings . [ATTACHMENT filename='use-project-dialog-2.png']
5. Use the default settings and click Finish . The simulation profile will be added to the project. You can see it in the containment browser. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddavd4057523d3d2f3e88e8ca3eb06273df0.png']

An Simulation Configuration is a Class Element, which is applied with the «SimulationConfig» stereotype (see the following figure). This stereotype contains tag definitions to customize model execution (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SimulationConfig stereotype' space='CST2024x']).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, we will create the Simulation Configuration to execute our StopWatch model without using animation. 
 
 To execute our StopWatch model without using animation

1. Open a System Class diagram.
2. Create a new Class Element by clicking the Class button on the Class diagram toolbar.
3. Click the diagram to create the Class Element.
4. Name the created Class Element "SilentStopwatch" .
5. Right-click the SilentStopwatch Class and select Stereotype to apply the «SimulationConfig» stereotype to the created Class. The Stereotype context menu group will open. [ATTACHMENT filename='Applying SimulationConfig Stereotype.png']
6. Select SimulationConfig from the list of available stereotypes for the Class Element.
7. Click Apply . The SilentStopwatch Class will be with the applied SimulationConfig stereotype. [ATTACHMENT filename='Applying SimulationConfig Stereotype Completed.png']

Next, we will create a SimulationLog to record the runtime events occurrence and assign this SimulationLog to the SimulationConfig.

To record all runtime events occurrence and assign a SimulationLog to the SimulationConfig

1. Create a new Class Element by clicking the Class button on the Class diagram toolbar.
2. Click the diagram to create the Class Element.
3. Name the created Class "SimulationLogs" .
4. Right-click the SimulationLogs Class and select Stereotype to apply the «SimulationLog» stereotype to the created Class. The Stereotype context menu will open. [ATTACHMENT filename='Applying SimulationLog Stereotype.png']
5. Select SimulationLog and click Apply . The SimulationLogs Class will be with the applied «SimulationLog» stereotype. [ATTACHMENT filename='SimulationLog Completed.png']

Now we need to specify the tagged values of the «SimulationConfig» stereotype that is applied to the SilentStopwatch Class. 
 
To specify the tagged values of the «SimulationConfig» stereotype

1. Open the Specification dialog of the SilentStopwatch Simulation Configuration by right-clicking the SilentStopwatch symbol and select Specification .
2. Select the Tags node on the left-hand side of the dialog.
3. Select SimulationProfile from the Profile drop-down menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='Specification Dialog Selecting SimulationProfile.png']
4. Specify the tagged value of the execution target to the StopWatch Class by selecting executionTarget and click Create Value . The Select Elements dialog will open for you to select the Element that will be the execution target. [ATTACHMENT filename='Select Elements Dialog for executionTarget.png']
5. Select the StopWatch Class (you can also select the stopwatch InstanceSpecification, which has been created in the previous section) as the executionTarget .
6. Click OK to close the Select Elements dialog.
7. Follow the same steps used to set the tagged value of executionTarget to the StopWatch Class and specify the tagged values of the following tag definitions i. log to SimulationLogs . ii. resultLocation to the Stopwatch folder. iii. silent to true .
8. Click Close to close the Specification dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='Complete SimulationConfig.png']

To execute the Simulation Configuration

1. Right-click the SilentStopwatch symbol on the diagram and select Simulation > Execute .
2. Click the Run Simulation button on the Simulation window toolbar to start the simulation. The StopWatch Class will then be executed without animation. You will need to wait until the runtime object of StopWatch is at the ready State. You can see the State of the runtime object at the Variables tab. The State of the object will be displayed in square brackets.
3. Select the StopWatch node in the Variables tab.
4. Select the start signal from the Triggers drop-down menu on the Simulation window toolbar.

Now, you can see that the value of **time:Integer**, which is displayed in the **Variables** tab, will increase by one every second. 
 
To stop the execution

1. Select the StopWatch node in the Variables tab.
2. Select the stop signal from the Triggers drop-down menu. The StopWatch object will go to the stopped State.
3. Select the stop signal again to move the object to the final stage to close the simulation session. The latest value of time:Integer will be saved into the slot value of the stopwatch InstanceSpecification.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to customize model execution by using an Simulation Configuration. The Simulation Configuration is a stereotype that can be applied to a Class Element. This stereotype contains tag definitions for model execution customization such as enable or disable animation and UI mockups. To be able to use the Simulation Configuration, we need to add the <em>SimulationProfile.mdzip</em> file to our project. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To add the <em>SimulationProfile.mdzip</em> file to a project</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Use</strong> <strong>Project</strong> on the main menu. The <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Stereotype</strong> context menu group will open.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Applying SimulationConfig Stereotype.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Executing the StopWatch using Simulation Configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">SimulationConfig</strong> from the list of available stereotypes for the Class Element.</li><li>Click <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Apply</strong>. The SilentStopwatch Class will be with the applied SimulationConfig stereotype.<br /><br />  <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Applying SimulationConfig Stereotype Completed.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Executing the StopWatch using Simulation Configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> Next, we will create a SimulationLog to record the runtime events occurrence and assign this SimulationLog to the SimulationConfig. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>To record all runtime events occurrence and assign a SimulationLog to the SimulationConfig</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a new Class Element by clicking the <strong>Class</strong> button on the Class diagram toolbar.</li><li>Click the diagram to create the Class Element.</li><li>Name the created Class <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">&quot;SimulationLogs&quot;</em>.</li><li>Right-click the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">SimulationLogs</strong> Class and select <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Stereotype</strong> to apply the «SimulationLog» stereotype to the created Class. <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">log</strong> to <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">SimulationLogs</strong>. <br />ii. <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">resultLocation</strong> to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Stopwatch</strong> folder.<br />iii. <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">silent</strong> to <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">true</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Close</strong> to close the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Specification</strong> dialog.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Complete SimulationConfig.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Executing the StopWatch using Simulation Configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To execute the Simulation Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the <strong>SilentStopwatch</strong> symbol on the diagram and select <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Execute</strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Run</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Simulation</strong> button on the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Simulation</strong> window toolbar to start the simulation. The <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">StopWatch</strong> Class will then be executed without animation. You will need to wait until the runtime object of <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">StopWatch</strong> is at the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">ready</strong> State. You can see the State of the runtime object at the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Variables</strong> tab. The State of the object will be displayed in square brackets.</li><li>Select the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">StopWatch</strong> node in the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Variables</strong> tab.</li><li>Select the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">start</strong> signal from the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Triggers</strong> drop-down menu on the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Simulation</strong> window toolbar. </li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> Now, you can see that the value of <strong>time:Integer</strong>, which is displayed in the <strong>Variables</strong> tab, will increase by one every second. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To stop the execution</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the <strong>StopWatch</strong> node in the <strong>Variables</strong> tab.</li><li>Select the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">stop</strong> signal from the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Triggers</strong> drop-down menu. The <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">StopWatch</strong> object will go to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">stopped</strong> State.</li><li>Select the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">stop</strong> signal again to move the object to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">final</strong> stage to close the simulation session. The latest value of <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">time:Integer</strong> will be saved into the slot value of the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">stopwatch</strong> InstanceSpecification.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729992 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00110: Execution

- page_id: `136729992`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729992/Execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:42.493+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Developer Guide > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

189375002

189375005

189375003

A simulation execution represents a model execution of a specific element. It is the top level of the model execution which contains running execution sessions and execution engines. A simulation execution will start when the specific element is executed (through the graphical user interface or Java API). The following APIs are available for handling executions.

- Start Execution
- Stop Execution
- Creating and Registering a New Execution Listener

189375000

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It is the top level of the model execution which contains running execution sessions and execution engines. A simulation execution will start when the specific element is executed (through the graphical user interface or Java API). The following APIs are available for handling executions.</p><ul><li>Start Execution</li><li>Stop Execution</li><li>Creating and Registering a New Execution Listener</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a4d73df-5fe5-450a-9d47-db9a2e2daedb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">189375000</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="64cdf2d4-a520-4580-b651-6ca415e0180c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729478 space=CST2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00111: Execution of incomplete or dummy models

- page_id: `136729478`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729478/Execution+of+incomplete+or+dummy+models
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-01-03T10:39:52.278+01:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Activity simulation
- labels: ['dummy-models', 'incomplete-model-execution']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

376786264

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

376786266

376786262

**On this page**

22

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

376786265

Previous versions of Cameo Simulation Toolkit did not support execution of incomplete or dummy models. From the 18.4 version and above, it will be possible to execute incomplete or dummy models.

Previously, the execution would halt. Now, if any input pin does not have an accompanying value, the Behavior of the Action will not be to halt execution, instead a null value token will be passed to that pin, or when simulating a **CallOperationAction** which does not have a target pin, a runtime object, which is the context of the Activity will be selected as the target. Null Value tokens do not trigger flow animation on triggered behaviors if the input pin multiplicity's lower bound is 0.

The information on this page describes the Behavior of an Action when any of the input or output pins, or any properties of the Action, are not specified or not present.

#### INFO: Supported Actions

Supported Actions

The Actions which are currently supported are as follows

- AddStructuralFeatureValueAction
- ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction
- CallBehaviorAction
- CallOperationAction
- SendSignalAction
- ValueSpecificationAction
- StartClassifierBehaviorAction and StateObjectBehaviorAction
- OpaqueAction

#### AddStructuralFeatureValueAction

An add structural feature value Action is a write structural feature Action for adding values to a structural feature. When any input pin is unspecified or empty, or when a **Structural Feature** is unspecified or does not exist, this Action's Behaviorwill have the rules as follows

- I f Object input pin is not specified, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used as the target.
- If Value input pin is not specified, this Action will be skipped.
- If Structural Feature is not specified or it does not exist in its own context, then this Action will be skipped.

If an **Object** input pin is empty or unspecified, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used to add the value to the **Structural Feature** of the context.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A demo showing the situation if an object pin is unspecified.

#### ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction

A read structural feature Action is a structural feature Action that retrieves the values of a structural feature. When any of the input or output pins are unspecified or not present, this Action's Behaviorwill have the rules as follows

- If the Object pin is not specified, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used as the target.
- If Structural Feature is not specified or if the Structural Feature has no values, then a n ull value token will be sent.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A demo showing the situation if an object pin is not specified.

#### CallBehaviorAction

Call Behavior Action is a call Action that invokes a Behavior directly rather than invoking an operation that invokes the Behavior. When any of the **Argument** pins are either unspecified or not present, this Action's Behaviorwill have the rules as follows

- If any of the Argument input pins are unspecified, this Action will send a null value token for that parameter.
- If none of the Argument pins are unspecified, this Action will send null value tokens for all the Arguments .
- If the Result output pin is specified and this Action does not return any results, it will return a null value token instead.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A demo showing the situation if an argument pin is unspecified (A null value token is passed to the pin.).

#### CallOperationAction

A call operation Action is an Action that transmits an operation call request to the target object, where it may cause the invocation of associated Behavior. When any input or output pin is unspecified or not present, this Action's Behaviorwill have the rules as follows

- This Action will send a null value token for each unspecified Argument input pin.
- In case no Argument pin is specified, this Action will send null value tokens for all the parameters.
- If the **Result** output pin is specified and this Action does not return any results, it will return a **null** value token instead.
- If the **Target** input pin is not specified, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used as the target. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] A demo showing the situation if an argument input pin is unspecified (A null value token will be passed.).

#### SendSignalAction

Send signal Action is an invocation Action that creates a signal from its inputs, and transmits it to the specified target object, where it may cause the firing of a State Machine transition or the execution of an Activity. When either **Argument** pin or **Target** input pin is unspecified or empty, this Action's Behavior will have the rules as follows

- If there are any incomplete Argument pins, this Action will send null value tokens for those parameters.
- If Argument pin is not present, this Action will send null value tokens for all parameters.
- If Target input pin is not present, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used as the target.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A demo showing the situation when there are incomplete argument pins.

#### ValueSpecificationAction

A value specification Action is an Action that evaluates a value specification. When the value of a **ValueSpecification** Action is not specified, this Action's Behavior will apply the rule as follows

- If the value of a ValueSpecification Action is not specified, a **null** value token will be passed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A demo showing the situation when the value of a ValueSpecification Action is unspecified (A null value token is passed.).

#### StartClassifierBehaviorAction and StateObjectBehaviorAction

A start Classifier Behavior Action is an Action that starts the Classifier Behavior of the input. If the **Object** input pin is not present or empty, this Action's Behavior will apply the rule as follows

- If the Object input pin is not present or empty, the StartClassifierBehaviorAction Action will use the runtime context of the Activity execution as the target.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A demo showing the situation when the object pin is either not present or empty.

#### OpaqueAction

Opaque Actions are a type of Action that can be used to represent implementation information. They can also be used as placeholder Actions until you determine the specific type of Action to use. If the opaque Action does not return a value to the **OutputValue** pin, this Action's Behavior will apply the rule as follows

- If the opaque Action does not return any value to the OutputValue pin, a null value token will be passed to the pin at runtime.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### If an opaque Action does not return any value to the OutputValue pin, a null token will be passed to the pin.

#### INFO: Information

Information

A **"null value token"** is an **ObjectToken** with has no values defined.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e8fe2e6-3547-4435-8ac7-06a67d51bef6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">376786264</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6da8c9f3-723f-4700-b00a-441d2a9480ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">376786266</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea691c5f-b691-4c2d-811b-6ddf7a53906c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">376786262</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ceba2bf-9727-4011-bab3-bf9986f9d9c7"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">2</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e98163ce-e7a8-4dbf-bc13-a989207bd634"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">376786265</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Previous versions of Cameo Simulation Toolkit did not support execution of incomplete or dummy models. From the 18.4 version and above, it will be possible to execute incomplete or dummy models.</p><p>Previously, the execution would halt. Now, if any input pin does not have an accompanying value, the Behavior of the Action will not be to halt execution, instead a null value token will be passed to that pin, or when simulating a <strong>CallOperationAction</strong> which does not have a target pin, a runtime object, which is the context of the Activity will be selected as the target. <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Null Value tokens do not trigger flow animation on triggered behaviors if the input pin multiplicity's lower bound is 0.</span></p><p>The information on this page describes the Behavior of an Action when any of the input or output pins, or any properties of the Action, are not specified or not present.</p><div><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f37e438-92d0-413e-8e9b-4efad84cc93c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Supported Actions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Actions which are currently supported are as follows<br /></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">AddStructuralFeatureValueAction</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">CallBehaviorAction</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">CallOperationAction</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">SendSignalAction</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ValueSpecificationAction</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">StartClassifierBehaviorAction and StateObjectBehaviorAction</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">OpaqueAction</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h2>AddStructuralFeatureValueAction<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.42857;"> </span></h2><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An add structural feature value Action is a write structural feature Action for adding values to a structural feature. When any input pin is unspecified or empty, or when a <strong>Structural Feature</strong> is unspecified or does not exist, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">this Action's Behavior </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">will have the rules as follows</span></span><br /></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">I</span>f <strong>Object</strong> input pin is not specified, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used as the target.</li><li>If <strong>Value</strong> input pin is not specified, this Action will be skipped. </li><li>If <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Structural Feature</strong> is not specified or it does not exist in its own context, then this Action will be skipped. </li></ul><p><br />If an <strong>Object</strong> input pin is empty or unspecified, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used to add the value to the <strong>Structural Feature</strong> of the context.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1.AddStructuralFeatureValueAction.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A demo showing the situation if an object pin is unspecified.</h6><h2>ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction</h2><p>A read structural feature Action is a structural feature Action that retrieves the values of a structural feature. When any of the input or output pins are unspecified or not present, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">this Action's Behavior </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">will have the rules as follows</span></span></span></span></p><ul><li>If the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Object</strong> pin is not specified, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used as the target.</li><li>If <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Structural Feature</strong> is not specified or if the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Structural Feature</strong> has no values, then a <strong>n</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">ull</strong> value token will be sent.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="2.ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A demo showing the situation if an object pin is not specified.</h6><h2>CallBehaviorAction</h2><p>Call Behavior Action is a call Action that invokes a Behavior directly rather than invoking an operation that invokes the Behavior. When any of the <strong>Argument</strong> pins are either unspecified or not present, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">this Action's Behavior </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">will have the rules as follows</span></span></span></p><ul><li>If any of the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Argument</strong> input pins are unspecified, this Action will send a <strong>null</strong> value token for that parameter. </li><li>If none of the <strong>Argument</strong> pins are unspecified, this Action will send <strong>null</strong> value tokens for all the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Arguments</strong>. </li><li>If the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Result</strong> output pin is specified and this Action does not return any results, it will return a <strong>null</strong> value token instead.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3.CallBehaviorAction.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A demo showing the situation if an argument pin is unspecified (A null value token is passed to the pin.).</h6><h2>CallOperationAction </h2><p>A call operation Action is an Action that transmits an operation call request to the target object, where it may cause the invocation of associated Behavior. When any input or output pin is unspecified or not present, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">this Action's Behavior </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">will have the rules as follows</span></span></span></p><ul><li>This Action will send a null value token for each unspecified <strong>Argument</strong> input pin. </li><li>In case no <strong>Argument</strong> pin is specified, this Action will send <strong>null</strong> value tokens for all the parameters. </li><li><p>If the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Result</strong> output pin is specified and this Action does not return any results, it will return a <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">null</strong> value token instead.</p></li><li><p>If the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Target</strong> input pin is not specified, the runtime context of the Activity execution  will be used as the target.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="4.CallOperationAction.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A demo showing the situation if an argument input pin is unspecified (A null value token will be passed.).</h6></li></ul><h2>SendSignalAction<span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> </span></h2><p>Send signal Action is an invocation Action that creates a signal from its inputs, and transmits it to the specified target object, where it may cause the firing of a State Machine transition or the execution of an Activity. When either <strong>Argument</strong> pin or <strong>Target</strong> input pin is unspecified or empty, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">this Action's Behavior will have the rules as follows</span></span></p><ul><li>If there are any incomplete <strong>Argument</strong> pins, this Action will send null value tokens for those parameters.</li><li>If <strong>Argument</strong> pin is not present, this Action will send null value tokens for all parameters.</li><li>If <strong>Target</strong> input pin is not present, the runtime context of the Activity execution will be used as the target. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="5.SendSignalAction.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A demo showing the situation when there are incomplete argument pins.</h6><h2>ValueSpecificationAction<span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> </span></h2><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">A value specification Action is an Action that evaluates a value specification. When the value of a <strong>ValueSpecification</strong> Action is not specified, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">this Action's Behavior will apply the rule as follows</span></span><br /></span></p><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">If the value of a </span><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">ValueSpecification</strong><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> Action is not specified, a <strong>null</strong> value token will be passed.<br /></span></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="6.ValueSpecificationAction.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A demo showing the situation when the value of a ValueSpecification Action is unspecified (A null value token is passed.).</h6><h2>StartClassifierBehaviorAction and StateObjectBehaviorAction </h2><p>A start Classifier Behavior Action is an Action that starts the Classifier Behavior of the input. If the <strong>Object</strong> input pin is not present or empty, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">this Action's Behavior will apply the rule as follows</span></span></p><ul><li>If the <strong>Object</strong> input pin is not present or empty, the <strong>StartClassifierBehaviorAction</strong> Action will use the runtime context of the Activity execution as the target.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="7.StartClassifierBehaviorAction.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> A demo showing the situation when the object pin is either not present or empty.</h6><h2>OpaqueAction</h2><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Opaque Actions are a type of Action that can be used to represent implementation information. They can also be used as placeholder Actions until you determine the specific type of Action to use. If the opaque Action does not return a value to the <strong>OutputValue</strong> pin, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">this Action's Behavior will apply the rule as follows</span></span><br /></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> If the opaque Action does not return any value to the </span><strong>OutputValue</strong> pin, a <strong>null</strong> value token will be passed to the pin at runtime.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="8.OpaqueAction.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">If an opaque Action does not return any value to the OutputValue pin, a null token will be passed to the pin.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="307ac12b-77b0-4277-87ed-2492ff681cf7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <strong>&quot;null value token&quot;</strong> is an <strong>ObjectToken</strong> with has no values defined.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136728885 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00112: Exporting plots data

- page_id: `136728885`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136728885/Exporting+plots+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:58:42.308+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > Time Series Chart
- labels: ['plot-data', 'exporting-plot-data']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415232085

1415232088

1415232086

A Time series chart provides two options for you to export your plot data in which time values (the values that appear on the horizontal axis) and the corresponding recorded values (the values that appear on the vertical axis) are plotted in a Time series chart. You can export the plot values to either a comma-separated value file (CSV file) or the slot values of an instance model.

1415232084

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7757e2c1-82c4-4192-8071-52c0a7bba4c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415232085</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e35daef-221d-491b-9354-392e9172974b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415232088</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dba503c-636f-4036-9b3d-3b93e9ba67f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415232086</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Time series chart provides two options for you to export your plot data in which time values (the values that appear on the horizontal axis) and the corresponding recorded values (the values that appear on the vertical axis) are plotted in a Time series chart. You can export the plot values to either a comma-separated value file (CSV file) or the slot values of an instance model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3f58372-71f4-439c-b558-4028a99cdab5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415232084</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c68ec771-4d65-4891-a7e5-50e55a78cc79" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136728891 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00113: Exporting plots data to a CSV file

- page_id: `136728891`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136728891/Exporting+plots+data+to+a+CSV+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:58:42.616+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > Time Series Chart > Exporting plots data
- labels: ['plot-data-to-csv-file', 'exporting-plot-data-to-csv']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415448634

1415448636

1415448635

The values plotted in a Time series chart are exportable to a CSV file.

To export plots data of a Time series chart to a CSV file

1. Click the **Export** **Data** toolbar button on the Time series chart's plot panel. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Enter a filename and select a location to save the file.
3. Click the Save button. The following figure shows plotted values in the Time series chart

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Plotted values in the Time series chart.

The following figure shows the exported plot values in a CSV file

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exported plot values in a CSV file.

If you exported the plotted values in a Time series chart to a CSV file, the exported plots data in the CSV file would look like those shown in the first figure. The axes labels would appear in the first paragraph of the file as the column header.

1415448633

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting plots data' space='CST2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136728893 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00114: Exporting plots data to an instance model

- page_id: `136728893`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136728893/Exporting+plots+data+to+an+instance+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:58:42.768+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > Time Series Chart > Exporting plots data
- labels: ['exporting-plot-data-to-instance-model', 'plot-data-to-instance-model', 'auto-save-plot-values']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415823846

1415823848

1415823847

Cameo Simulation Toolkit also allows you to export plots data in a Time series chart to an Instance model. The model for exported plots data in the simulation profile comprises of DataSet and Data Classes. Whenever you export plots data to an instance model, Cameo Simulation Toolkit will create an InstanceSpecification of a DataSet Class. The slot of a data property will contain a list of InstanceSpecifications of the Data Class. The first instance on the list is an InstanceSpecification containing the time values while the second one is an InstanceSpecification containing the values that appear on the vertical axis of the first plot. If a Time series chart has more than one plot, the third InstanceSpecification on the list will be an InstanceSpecification containing the values of the second plot and so on.

To export plots data to an instance model

1. Click the **Export** **to** **Instance** toolbar button of the Time series chart's panel whose values you want to export. The **Element Selection** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Select a package that will own the instance model you want to create.
3. Click the OK button. Cameo Simulation Toolkit will create the instance model containing the plots data in a Time Series Chart as shown in the following figure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The instance model containing the plots data in a Time series chart.

To auto-save plot values into model on every simulation run

1. Do one of the following

- Double-click the Time series chart.
- Right-click the Time series chart and select Specification .

The Specification window will open as follows

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button beside the **Plot Result Location** property to edit its value. The**Plot Result Location** property value takes the name of the**Instance** to save the values, as in the following figure

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Start the simulation. When the simulation terminates, the plot values will be written to the **Instance specification** selected in the previous step, as in the following figure

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

1415823845

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting plots data' space='CST2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting plots data to a CSV file' space='CST2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729258 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00115: Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications

- page_id: `136729258`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729258/Exporting+runtime+objects+to+InstanceSpecifications
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:03.071+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation debugging > Runtime Value Monitoring
- labels: ['exporting-runtime-values', 'exporting-runtime-objects']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

45332699

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

45332701

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

45332700

You can export the values of a runtime object through the **Variables** pane to the following Specifications

- a new InstanceSpecification.
- an existing InstanceSpecification.
- the InstanceSpecification that created the runtime object. Once exported, the values of a runtime object will be set to the slots of the InstanceSpecification.

The type of InstanceSpecification you want to export the values of a runtime object to, depends on the element you used to create the runtime object. If the element is a Classifier, you can export the values of the runtime object to either of the following

- A new InstanceSpecification.
- An existing InstanceSpecification.

If the element is an InstanceSpecification, you can export the values of the runtime object to either of the following

- A new InstanceSpecification.
- The same InstanceSpecification that you used to create that runtime object.

To export a runtime value to a new InstanceSpecification

1. Do either of the following

- Click a runtime object whose value you want to export in the Name column and click the Export to New Instance icon on the Variables pane toolbar.
- Right-click the row and select Export Value To > New Instance

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. The **Select Owner** dialog will open. Select the owner of the new InstanceSpecification (the system folder).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Click **OK**.

To export a runtime value to an existing InstanceSpecification

1. Do either of the following

- Click a runtime object whose value you want to export in the Name column and click the Export to Instance icon on the Variables pane toolbar.
- Right-click the row and select Export Value To > Instance .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

To export the value of a runtime object using this method, the runtime object must be created from a Classifier.

2. The **Select Instance** dialog will open. Select an InstanceSpecification that will be used to save the runtime object (You can select only the InstanceSpecification whose classifier is the same as that of the runtime object). 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Click **OK**.

To export a runtime value to the InstanceSpecification that has created the runtime object

1. Do either of the following

- Click a runtime object whose value you want to export in the Name column and click the Export to Instance icon on the Variables pane toolbar.
- Right-click the row and select Export Value To > Instance .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. Once Cameo Simulation Toolkit exported and saved the values of the runtime object to the Instance Specification that created it, you can see the hyperlink to that particular Instance Specification in the **Simulation** **Console** pane.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Click the hyperlink, and you will see the exact Instance Specification in the Containment tree.

This method allows you to export the value of the runtime object to the slots of the Instance Specification that has created the object. You can see the notification of each successful export and a location hyperlink, if any, in the **Simulation** **Console** pane.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

To export the value of a runtime object using this method, the runtime object must be created from an Instance Specification.

45332698

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='CST2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation console' space='CST2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Variables pane' space='CST2024x']

Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p>To export a runtime value to an existing InstanceSpecification</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Do either of the following</p></li></ol><ul><li>Click a runtime object whose value you want to export in the <strong>Name</strong> column and click the <strong>Export</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Instance</strong> icon on the <strong>Variables </strong>pane toolbar.</li><li>Right-click the row and select <strong>Export</strong> <strong>Value</strong> <strong>To</strong> &gt; <strong>Instance</strong>. </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /><ac:image ac:title="Exporting a Runtime Valuetoan Existing Instance through the Context Menu" ac:alt="Exporting a Runtime Valuetoan Existing Instance through the Context Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export_toExist_Inst.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d26878c-e8fa-4017-b2e2-51ed7ace483a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To export the value of a runtime object using this method, the runtime object must be created from a Classifier.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. The <strong>Select Instance</strong> dialog will open. Select an InstanceSpecification that will be used to save the runtime object (You can select only the InstanceSpecification whose classifier is the same as that of the runtime object).<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Selecting an InstanceSpecification in the Select Instance Dialog" ac:alt="Selecting an InstanceSpecification in the Select Instance Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select-instance-a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Click <strong>OK</strong>. </p><p><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"> </span></p><p>To export a runtime value to the InstanceSpecification that has created the runtime object</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Do either of the following</p></li></ol><ul><li>Click a runtime object whose value you want to export in the <strong>Name</strong> column and click the <strong>Export</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Instance</strong> icon on the <strong>Variables</strong> pane toolbar.</li><li>Right-click the row and select <strong>Export</strong> <strong>Value</strong> <strong>To</strong> &gt; <strong>Instance</strong>. </li></ul><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Exporting a Runtime Value to the Creating Instance through the Context Menu" ac:alt="Exporting a Runtime Value to the Creating Instance through the Context Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export_toCreatorInstance.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. Once Cameo Simulation Toolkit exported and saved the values of the runtime object to the Instance Specification that created it, you can see the hyperlink to that particular Instance Specification in the <strong>Simulation</strong> <strong>Console</strong> pane.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Successful Export Notification and Hyperlink" ac:alt="Successful Export Notification and Hyperlink"><ri:attachment ri:filename="110150_2016-06-28+12_07_54-Greenshot.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Click the hyperlink, and you will see the exact Instance Specification in the Containment tree.</p><p>This method allows you to export the value of the runtime object to the slots of the Instance Specification that has created the object. You can see the notification of each successful export and a location hyperlink, if any, in the <strong>Simulation</strong> <strong>Console</strong> pane. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a159bd45-9c64-400a-a3c9-db306df49176"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To export the value of a runtime object using this method, the runtime object must be created from an Instance Specification.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f7485fa-3963-4103-a495-e6f04f35f800"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">45332698</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Simulation console" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Variables pane" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729853 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00116: Extraction of Constraints from Text Based Requirements

- page_id: `136729853`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729853/Extraction+of+Constraints+from+Text+Based+Requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:37.914+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Requirements traceability from the Variables pane
- labels: ['extracting-constraints-from-text-based-requirements']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

As of version 18.4, another new addition to Cameo Simulation Toolkit's already impressive repertoire, is the ability to automatically extract constraint equations from the text of a requirement. 
For example, if a requirement text states that a moving car must have speed more than 0, then the constraint equation “speed > 0” is automatically extracted from the requirement text and will be evaluated upon running of a simulation. 
While typing the requirement text, a shortcut menu appears as soon as you type some keywords which are available in the Glossary, see the example as follows

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Constraints within text-based requirements.

For the constraint to execute properly, the requirement should be linked to a property, such as in the above example, a satisfy relation is used to link between the property and the requirement.

When the simulation is run, the constraint is evaluated and color-coded according to the result of the simulation, red if the constraint fails and green if it passes, as shown in figure below. Additionally, a mouse over the variable will display a tooltip.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Running a simulation evaluates the constraint within the requirement and color-codes it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>As of version 18.4, another new addition to Cameo Simulation Toolkit's already impressive repertoire, is the ability to automatically extract constraint equations from the text of a requirement.<br />For example, if a requirement text states that a moving car must have speed more than 0, then the constraint equation “speed &gt; 0” is automatically extracted from the requirement text and will be evaluated upon running of a simulation.<br />While typing the requirement text, a shortcut menu appears as soon as you type some keywords which are available in the Glossary, see the example as follows</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Constraints within text based  requirements" ac:alt="Constraints within text based  requirements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="108924_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Extraction of Constraints from Text Based Requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Constraints within text-based requirements.</h6><p>For the constraint to execute properly, the requirement should be linked to a property, such as in the above example, a satisfy relation is used to link between the property and the requirement.</p><p>When the simulation is run, the constraint is evaluated and color-coded according to the result of the simulation, red if the constraint fails and green if it passes, as shown in figure below. Additionally, a mouse over the variable will display a tooltip. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Running a simulation evaluates the constraint within the requirement and color-codes it" ac:alt="Running a simulation evaluates the constraint within the requirement and color-codes it"><ri:attachment ri:filename="108925_b.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Extraction of Constraints from Text Based Requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Running a simulation evaluates the constraint within the requirement and color-codes it.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729827 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00117: Flow Port

- page_id: `136729827`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729827/Flow+Port
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:34.493+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements > Port
- labels: ['flow-port']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

944174807

944174818

944174808

A [Flow Port](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024x/Flow+Port) is a Port that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment. Flow updating depends on directions (**in**, **out**, and **inout** directions as shown in the figure below). If there is the **in** or **out** direction, the value cannot be updated in the reverse direction. In general, Flow Ports are used for asynchronous, broadcast, and send-and-forget interactions. Animation of the value flow is animated through parts, Ports, and Connectors. Both atomic Flow Ports (typed by Signal or ValueType) and non-atomic Flow Ports (typed by FlowSpecification) are supported. If the Flow Port is connected to multiple external and/or internal Connectors, the items are propagated (broadcast) to the other ends of all Connectors that have matching properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Flow Ports with animation of value propagation in the Internal Block diagram.

944174806

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='CST2024x']
- Full Port
- Proxy Port

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7f440aa-f244-46c4-883a-da53d49e1d67"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944174807</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e60bd445-1abc-4057-a8f3-dcf891f706a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944174818</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ae9ba71-5c7e-440b-bbd3-7c901cbc9161"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944174808</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024x/Flow+Port">Flow Port</a> is a Port that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment. Flow updating depends on directions (<strong>in</strong>, <strong>out</strong>, and <strong>inout</strong> directions as shown in the figure below). If there is the <strong>in</strong> or <strong>out</strong> direction, the value cannot be updated in the reverse direction. In general, Flow Ports are used for asynchronous, broadcast, and send-and-forget interactions. Animation of the value flow is animated through parts, Ports, and Connectors. Both atomic Flow Ports (typed by Signal or ValueType) and non-atomic Flow Ports (typed by FlowSpecification) are supported. If the Flow Port is connected to multiple external and/or internal Connectors, the items are propagated (broadcast) to the other ends of all Connectors that have matching properties.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Flow Port.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Flow Port" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Flow Ports with animation of value propagation in the Internal Block diagram.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f841c696-fc2d-400d-b74d-5813886768fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944174806</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Full+Port">Full Port</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Proxy+Port">Proxy Port</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729822 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00118: Flow property

- page_id: `136729822`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729822/Flow+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:33.834+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['flow-property']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A flow property is a property to which the stereotype «FlowProperty» is applied. A flow property has a flow direction. A flow due to flow properties will incur flow properties matching. Matching flow properties must have the same direction and type. If the direction and type of multiple flow properties at either end of a connector match, a flow will occur between the flow properties with the same name. When the flow occurs between matching flow properties, the value of the flow property with a direction 'out' or 'inout' will be delivered to the flow property with the direction 'in' or 'inout'.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A flow property is a property to which the stereotype «FlowProperty» is applied. A flow property has a flow direction. A flow due to flow properties will incur flow properties matching. Matching flow properties must have the same direction and type. If the direction and type of multiple flow properties at either end of a connector match, a flow will occur between the flow properties with the same name. When the flow occurs between matching flow properties, the value of the flow property with a direction 'out' or 'inout' will be delivered to the flow property with the direction 'in' or 'inout'. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729796 space=CST2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00119: FMI 2.0 co-simulation

- page_id: `136729796`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729796/FMI+2.0+co-simulation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-22T07:23:05.107+01:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models
- labels: ['fmi-co-simulation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports Functional Mockup Interface (FMI) Version 1.0 and 2.0. FMI 2.0 co-simulation applies when several FMIs are connected in Internal Block diagrams (IBD), an integration of separate independent black box simulation models along with the master algorithm.

FMI is a standard that supports model exchange and co-simulation of models. A component that implements FMI is called FMU (Functional Mockup Unit). Cameo Simulation Toolkit is also capable of reading FMU files. An FMU is a zipped file containing an XML description file and implementation in binary form that executes the equation representing the component’s Behavior.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Before simulating FMU files, you must make sure the following conditions are met:

- The FMU file can be attached to the project, placed in the same directory as the project or the path to the file can be specified as url tag on the FMU block. The path is set automatically when importing an FMU file. [ATTACHMENT filename='File path .png']
- The FMU files must be OS-platform supported, e.g., Win64, Win32, linux64, linux32, etc. (Cross-platform execution of FMU files is not supported.). You can see all supported platforms in the Binaries folders of the FMU files. If the platform you are using is not in the Binaries folder, it will not be supported.
- An FMU Block requires a SimulationConfig with the Internal simulation clock to provide the reference time in the simulation (shown in Step 3 below). Otherwise, a warning message will be shown in the Console pane. Please see also the WaterTankFMI build-in sample of Simulation.

To co-simulate FMU Blocks

1. Drag FMU files into a Block Definition diagram of the SysML project. The FMU files will be presented as «block» and «fmu» stereotypes along with ports. You may create associations (aggregations) with the main system block to make the FMU files become part properties.
2. If there is only one FMU file to simulate, proceed to Step 3. Otherwise, you will have to create an Internal Block diagram for the main system block to automatically include part properties in the diagram. Select the Flow Port context menu to show flow ports of those properties and create connections as needed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Create a Simulation Configuration diagram with a [SimulationConfig](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/SimulationConfig+stereotype) to specify *startTime, stepSize*, and *endTime* (optional) in the properties. You can add a [Time series chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Time+series+chart) and specify *represents* and *value* of the FMU represented as user interfaces. You can also include [CSVExport](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/CSV+export) to store the results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

4. Run the Simulation Configuration and see the results in the Time series chart and **Variables** pane, as well as the resulting Excel file from CSVExport.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="Textbody">Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports Functional Mockup Interface (FMI) Version 1.0 and 2.0. FMI 2.0 co-simulation applies when several FMIs are connected in Internal Block diagrams (IBD), an integration of separate independent black box simulation models along with the master algorithm.</p><p class="Textbody">FMI is a standard that supports model exchange and co-simulation of models. A component that implements FMI is called FMU (Functional Mockup Unit). Cameo Simulation Toolkit is also capable of reading FMU files. An FMU is a zipped file containing an XML description file and implementation in binary form that executes the equation representing the component’s Behavior.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a71e4f85-9e60-4ce5-9c23-3378be2dc56a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="Textbody">Before simulating FMU files, you must make sure the following conditions are met:</p><ul><li>The FMU file can be attached to the project, placed in the same directory as the project or the path to the file can be specified as url tag on the FMU block. The path is set automatically when importing an FMU file.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="File path .png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>The FMU files must be OS-platform supported, e.g., Win64, Win32, linux64, linux32, etc. (Cross-platform execution of FMU files is not supported.). You can see all supported platforms in the <strong>Binaries</strong> folders of the FMU files. If the platform you are using is not in the <strong>Binaries</strong> folder, it will not be supported.</li><li>An FMU Block requires a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/SimulationConfig+stereotype">SimulationConfig</a> with the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Internal+simulation+clock">Internal simulation clock</a> to provide the reference time in the simulation (shown in Step 3 below). Otherwise, a warning message will be shown in the <strong>Console</strong> pane. Please see also the <strong>WaterTankFMI</strong> build-in sample of Simulation.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To co-simulate FMU Blocks</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag FMU files into a Block Definition diagram of the SysML project. The FMU files will be presented as «block» and «fmu» stereotypes along with ports. You may create associations (aggregations) with the main system block to make the FMU files become part properties.</li><li>If there is only one FMU file to simulate, proceed to Step 3. Otherwise, you will have to create an Internal Block diagram for the main system block to automatically include part properties in the diagram. Select the <strong>Flow Port</strong> context menu to show flow ports of those properties and create connections as needed.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7113-1 Creating  a SysML Internal Block Diagram.PNG" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Create a Simulation Configuration diagram with a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/SimulationConfig+stereotype">SimulationConfig</a> to specify <em>startTime, stepSize</em>, and <em>endTime</em> (optional) in the properties. You can add a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Time+series+chart">Time series chart</a> and specify <em>represents</em> and <em>value</em> of the FMU represented as user interfaces. You can also include <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/CSV+export">CSVExport</a> to store the results.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7113-2 Creating a Simulation Configuration Diagram.PNG" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">4. Run the Simulation Configuration and see the results in the Time series chart and <strong>Variables</strong> pane, as well as the resulting Excel file from CSVExport.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7113-3 Running the Simulation Configuration.PNG" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729979 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00120: Frequently asked questions

- page_id: `136729979`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729979/Frequently+asked+questions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:41.973+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide
- labels: ['frequently-asked-questions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1155794510

1155794521

1155794509

**On this page**

1155794520

##### Activity simulation

**Question 1**:

Why isn't an operation outside a context object executed, e.g., an operation referring to an Activity outside a Block?

**Answer**: According to the fUML specification, an operation will be dispatched to the target object only, which is the context of the execution. You need to move the Activity into the Block and use it as a CallBehaviorAction with the Behavior setting.

**Question 2**:

Can I simulate dummy tokens of CallBehaviorActions that do not have a Behavior?

**Answer**: Yes, you can set the **Auto-Create fUML Object of Output Pin** option in the **Simulation [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='CST2024x']**dialog to **true**, which is the default value in Version 19.0 SP2 and later. Please see also****[Dummy tokens of Actions through Output Pins](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Dummy+tokens+of+Actions+through+Output+Pins).

##### Parametric evaluation

**Question 1**:

Why can't I use MATLAB as an external evaluator even after [integration with MATLAB](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Integration+with+MATLAB) of the modeling tool?

**Answer**: You must use the 64-bit version of MATLAB (Version 2012a or later) to align it with the 64-bit version of the modeling tool you are working with, e.g., MagicDraw or Cameo Systems Modeler. According to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using MATLAB on Microsoft Windows' space='CST2024x'], you must also ensure that **Path** of **System variables** in the **Environment Variables** dialog is set to the correct MATLAB path.

**Question 2**:

Can I use MATLAB 2019b as an external evaluator?

**Answer**: Yes, you can use MATLAB 2019b as an external evaluator. MATLAB R2019b has a new option, **Single simulation output**, set true by default in Simulink, which doesn’t affect the simulation. However, there are a few limitations as follows:

- If Simulation cannot load the Simulink, e.g., an invalid URL/ filename, both warning and script exception messages will be printed in the Console panel. In this case, the MATLAB engine cannot be used, so you will need to manually restart MATLAB.
- If there is no input for the In Port, Simulation will skip the evaluation, and a warning message will be printed in Console panel.

##### State Machine simulation

**Question**:

Can I change State activation semantics from **before entry** (official UML semantics) to **after entry**?

**Answer**: Yes, you can set the **State** **Activation** **Semantics** option in the **Simulation [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='CST2024x']**dialog to **After entry**. The default value is **Before entry** in Version 19.0 and later. Please see also [State activation semantics.](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/State+activation+semantics)

1775218708

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space='CST2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dummy tokens of Actions through Output Pins' space='CST2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de4adf70-1b0b-4eb8-8bed-afa6fb403dec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1155794510</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ccf0c90-ccff-43bf-a060-d506b1d812d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1155794521</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b424aca-9031-4ecf-ba3b-4dd88ceecd4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1155794509</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e37618d-f862-452f-ba10-5b92eb5a88e0" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6749c4a8-6a81-4078-8ffa-1aba66cc2892"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1155794520</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Activity simulation</h3><p><strong>Question 1</strong>:</p><p>Why isn't an operation outside a context object executed, e.g., an operation referring to an Activity outside a Block?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: According to the fUML specification, an operation will be dispatched to the target object only, which is the context of the execution. You need to move the Activity into the Block and use it as a CallBehaviorAction with the Behavior setting.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Question 2</strong>:</p><p>Can I simulate dummy tokens of CallBehaviorActions that do not have a Behavior?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: Yes, you can set the <strong>Auto-Create fUML Object of Output Pin</strong> option in the <strong>Simulation <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog to <strong>true</strong>, which is the default value in Version 19.0 SP2 and later. Please see also<strong> </strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Dummy+tokens+of+Actions+through+Output+Pins">Dummy tokens of Actions through Output Pins</a>.</p><p><br /></p><hr /><h3>Parametric evaluation</h3><p><strong>Question 1</strong>:</p><p>Why can't I use MATLAB as an external evaluator even after <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/Integration+with+MATLAB">integration with MATLAB</a> of the modeling tool?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: You must use the 64-bit version of MATLAB (Version 2012a or later) to align it with the 64-bit version of the modeling tool you are working with, e.g., MagicDraw or Cameo Systems Modeler. According to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Using MATLAB on Microsoft Windows" /></ac:link>, you must also ensure that <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Path</strong> of <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">System variables</strong> in the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Environment Variables</strong> dialog is set to the correct MATLAB path<span class="external-link" style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></p><p><span class="external-link"><br /></span></p><p><strong>Question 2</strong>:</p><p>Can I use MATLAB 2019b as an external evaluator?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: Yes, you can use MATLAB 2019b as an external evaluator. MATLAB R2019b has a new option, <strong>Single simulation output</strong>, set true by default in Simulink, which doesn’t affect the simulation. However, there are a few limitations as follows:</p><ul><li>If Simulation cannot load the Simulink, e.g., an invalid URL/ filename, both warning and script exception messages will be printed in the <strong>Console</strong> panel. In this case, the MATLAB engine cannot be used, so you will need to manually restart MATLAB.</li><li>If there is no input for the In Port, Simulation will skip the evaluation, and a warning message will be printed in <strong>Console</strong> panel.</li></ul><p><span class="external-link"><br /></span></p><hr /><h3>State Machine simulation</h3><p><strong>Question</strong>:</p><p>Can I change State activation semantics from <strong>before entry</strong> (official UML semantics) to <strong>after entry</strong>?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: Yes, you can set the <strong>State</strong> <strong>Activation</strong> <strong>Semantics</strong> option in the <strong>Simulation <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog to <strong>After entry</strong>. The default value is <strong>Before entry</strong> in Version 19.0 and later. Please see also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2024x/State+activation+semantics">State activation semantics.</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57e61c02-fb9a-4963-8dc0-ee001ec98766"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1775218708</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Activity simulation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Call Behavior Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Integration with MATLAB" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Parametric evaluator" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="State activation semantics" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Dummy tokens of Actions through Output Pins" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729829 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00121: Full Port

- page_id: `136729829`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729829/Full+Port
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:34.744+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements > Port
- labels: ['full-port']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

944179700

944179711

944179710

A Full port is a port stereotyped with «FullPort». It specifies a separate Element of an owning block. Initializing the owning block will cause Cameo Simulation Toolkit to initialize a port object from a Classifier that types the full port.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
An Object containing full Ports.

944179699

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='CST2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='CST2024x']
- Proxy Port

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
