You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting execution" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[start the execution]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stopping execution" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stop the execution]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and registering a new SimulationExecutionListener" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create and register a new Simulation Execution Listener]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> using Java APIs. Regarding the core component of the entire execution mechanism, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Engine" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[execution engine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defines how to execute a set of Element types. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="fUML Helper" /></ac:link> is also available as a Class that provides Helper methods related to the fUML structures. The Parametric engine also provides Java APIs for a parametric execution with a runtime object of a Classifier.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ff59532-b07a-49de-8b7d-553d3f24f7e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about Cameo Simulation Toolkit JavaDoc files, visit <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/simulation/package-summary.html">https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/simulation/package-summary.html</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7a755d7-e23f-4385-9a20-0c9ac57cb167"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">791502929</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="350125f7-b1e4-43ad-b7cd-09284b21119b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136728729 space=CST2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00153: Key features

- page_id: `136728729`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136728729/Key+features
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-03-14T10:39:23.638+01:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: ['key-features', 'main-features', 'features']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Simulation Toolkit is capable of running your UML or SysML models. The key features of Cameo Simulation Toolkit are as follows

- Simulation Framework The general infrastructure (including the simulation toolbars, context menu, and panes) and Open API for simulation.
- Activity Engine (fUML Engine) The OMG fUML (a foundational subset of the Executable UML) standard.
- State-Machine Engine (SCXML Engine) The W3C SCXML (State Charts XML) standard, which is an open-source Apache implementation.
- Interaction Engine Enabling Cameo Simulation Toolkit to simulate interactions that are modeled with Sequence diagrams.
- Parametric Engine Enabling Cameo Simulation Toolkit to simulate SysML Parametric diagrams.

The simulation sample projects are available in the *<*install_root*>/samples/simulation* directory.

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity simulation engine' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Cameo Simulation Toolkit is capable of running your UML or SysML models. The key features of Cameo Simulation Toolkit are as follows</p><ul><li><strong>Simulation</strong> <strong>Framework</strong> <br />The general infrastructure (including the simulation toolbars, context menu, and panes) and Open API for simulation.</li><li><strong>Activity Engine</strong> <strong>(fUML Engine)</strong> <br />The OMG fUML (a foundational subset of the Executable UML) standard.</li><li><strong>State-Machine Engine</strong> <strong>(SCXML Engine)</strong> <br />The W3C SCXML (State Charts XML) standard, which is an open-source Apache implementation.</li><li><strong>Interaction</strong> <strong>Engine</strong> <br />Enabling Cameo Simulation Toolkit to simulate interactions that are modeled with Sequence diagrams.</li><li><strong>Parametric Engine</strong> <br />Enabling Cameo Simulation Toolkit to simulate SysML Parametric diagrams. </li></ul><p>The simulation sample projects are available in the <em>&lt;<em style="text-align: left;">install_root</em>&gt;/samples/simulation</em> directory. </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity simulation engine" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729520 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00154: Lifeline

- page_id: `136729520`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729520/Lifeline
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:14.751+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Interaction simulation > Supported elements in interaction simulation
- labels: ['lifeline']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

1010481082

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

1010481084

1010481081

**On this page**

**33**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

1010481083

You can use a lifeline in a Sequence diagram to represent a property owned by a Class or a block that is the context of the diagram. Sending messages between objects specifying the properties presented by the lifelines can occur. Cameo Simulation Toolkit uses lifelines to find a source and a target object of the message.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Simulation console showing how lifelines can be used to find the source and target of a message.

You can use a lifeline in a SysML project to represent a nested property of a Class or a block, which is the context of a diagram. A path to the nested property appears as a dot notation on the lifeline symbol. Cameo Simulation Toolkit uses the path on the lifeline symbol to find the object.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Cameo Simulation Toolkit uses a lifeline with a dot notation to represent a nested property when you record a simulation as a Sequence diagram. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram' space='CST2024x'] for more information about recording a simulation.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Simulation console showing how a lifeline with a dot notation can represent a nested property.

Because Messages recorded in the system are not executed that they are internal mechanisms, Cameo Simulation Toolkit only executes Messages that are external to the system, e.g., a Message going from *User* Lifeline (without types) and *Found* Messages. All other Messages are not executed but verified and printed in the **Console** pane.

#### INFO: Information

Information

A *Found* Message is a Message where the occurrence of a receiving Event is known, but the occurrence of a sending Event is not.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Executing only external outgoing Messages, e.g., User Lifeline and Found Messages.

##### Storing nested property paths in a model

From version 2021x Refresh2, the Lifelines used to represent nested properties have the «NestedPropertyPath» stereotype applied to them. As a result, the path of a nested property is stored in the **propertyPath** tag of a Lifeline. Nested property paths of the Lifelines created with an earlier version of a modeling tool are stored in diagram symbols and you should save them in the model manually as described below.

To store nested property paths of Lifelines in a model

1. Right-click a Sequence Diagram in the Containment tree or an empty space in a Sequence Diagram.
2. In the context menu, select Refactor > Store Nested Property Paths in the Model . [ATTACHMENT filename='storing_nested_property_paths_in_model.png']

The nested property path of the selected Lifeline or the nested property paths of all Lifelines in the selected diagram are now stored in the model.

##### Lifeline selector

If a lifeline represents a part with the multiplicity of more than 1 (neither * nor 1..*), the list will also contain more than one object (not only one object) at runtime. The lifeline has the Selector expression that specifies the particular part represented by the lifeline.

Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the lifeline selector which allows specifying an exact object from the Selector expression. The Selector expression will be evaluated as an index integer to get an Object from the object collection.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The ledger[3] object is selected according to the index integer of lifeline Selector 3 of the Ledger object.

If the selector of a receiver is invalid or cannot be evaluated as an index integer, e.g., "X", none of the objects will be used, and a warning message will display in the simulation console.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25499c7f-9249-4ba6-9ce1-5c36da27b323"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1010481082</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0850e536-6317-448a-b379-246e79a80c97"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1010481084</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8985538e-15ce-45b5-8d8c-fd3f0f7e9012"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1010481081</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22833a8c-ad76-4544-ac98-21fc22209f26"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06a45f05-b7d0-4d89-ac03-0020e5cc5038"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1010481083</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use a lifeline in a Sequence diagram to represent a property owned by a Class or a block that is the context of the diagram. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Recording a Simulation as a Sequence Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more information about recording a simulation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Interaction Simulation with Lifelines Representing Nested Parts of a Diagram Context" ac:alt="Interaction Simulation with Lifelines Representing Nested Parts of a Diagram Context"><ri:attachment ri:filename="lifelines_repNestedparts.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Lifeline" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Simulation console showing how a lifeline with a dot notation can represent a nested property.</h6><p>Because Messages recorded in the system are not executed that they are internal mechanisms, Cameo Simulation Toolkit only executes Messages that are external to the system, e.g., a Message going from <em>User</em> Lifeline (without types) and <em>Found</em> Messages. Nested property paths of the Lifelines created with an earlier version of a modeling tool are stored in diagram symbols and you should save them in the model manually as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To store nested property paths of Lifelines in a model</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a Sequence Diagram in the Containment tree or an empty space in a Sequence Diagram.</li><li>In the context menu, select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Store Nested Property Paths in the Model</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="storing_nested_property_paths_in_model.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Lifeline" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><p>The nested property path of the selected Lifeline or the nested property paths of all Lifelines in the selected diagram are now stored in the model.<br /><br /></p><h3>Lifeline selector</h3><p class="Textbody">If a lifeline represents a part with the multiplicity of more than 1 (neither * nor 1..*), the list will also contain more than one object (not only one object) at runtime. The lifeline has the Selector expression that specifies the particular part represented by the lifeline.</p><p>Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the lifeline selector which allows specifying an exact object from the Selector expression. The Selector expression will be evaluated as an index integer to get an Object from the object collection.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Lifeline.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Lifeline" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The ledger[3] object is selected according to the index integer of lifeline Selector 3 of the Ledger object.</h6><p>If the selector of a receiver is invalid or cannot be evaluated as an index integer, e.g., &quot;X&quot;, none of the objects will be used, and a warning message will display in the simulation console.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136729671 space=CST2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00155: Logical expression

- page_id: `136729671`
- space_key: `CST2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CST2024x/pages/136729671/Logical+expression
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T07:59:22.851+02:00`
- ancestors: Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Parametric evaluator > Evaluating expressions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

402984464

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

402984466

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

402984465

A logical expression is an expression that uses comparison operators. You can use a logical expression to define a constraint. Cameo Simulation Toolkit uses the expression to validate values at runtime.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

A logical expression must contain a comparison operator (==, <, >, <=, >=, and <>) as shown in the following examples:

- val1 >= "500" is a valid expression.
- val2.equals("500") == true is a valid expression.
- val2.equals("500") is an invalid expression.

#### Red background for the labels and text fields in UI

If some constraints failed during evaluation, Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) will highlight the values in the **Variables** pane with a red background to denote that the values fail the constraint. All parent nodes (Name column) that contain the failed constraints will have a red background (until the root one in the tree view), which indicates failures deeper in the structure tree. You can also see these values in the **Simulation Console** pane.

Furthermore, both the values and validation that are highlighted must always be consistent with the labels and text boxes of both the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UI modeling diagram simulation' space='CST2024x'] and the HTML UI during simulation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Red backgrounds indicating failures during evaluation.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Neither green background nor tooltip will be used for "passed" verification status for both Swing mockups and HTML UI.

A tooltip will also appear if you hover the mouse over the failed constraint. Each parent of the failed constraint will appear with a contextual path "dot notation" starting from the node on which tooltip is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The parent node with a red background indicates failures deeper in the structure tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Nodes with green background indicating valid values.

This example uses the [ATTACHMENT filename='equationSysml.png']block **Circle** previously shown in the section [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mathematical equation' space='CST2024x']. If we modify the expression of the constraint block **Circle** **Area** by changing the assignment operator "**=**" to the equality operator "**==**", the parametric evaluator will use the expression to validate the values of both area and radius. If the result is false, the values will be highlighted in red (see Figure: The parent node with a red background indicates failures deeper in the structure tree). But, if the result is true, the values will be highlighted in green (see Figure: Nodes with green background indicating valid values).

#### Finding a constraint object that binds the value property

You can find the constraint object to which the value of the property is bound in the **Variables** pane.

To find a constraint object to which a value property is bound

- Right-click a constraint object row in the **Variables** pane, and select **Go** **To**. The constraint to which the value of the property is bound will appear, as shown in the figure below. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Finding a constraint object that binds the value property through the shortcut menu.

When you export the object to the instance specification, if the exported object contains a constraint object (an object of a constraint block) and if the expression of the constraint block is a boolean expression, Cameo Simulation Toolkit will set the result of the evaluation, which is a literal boolean, to a slot defined by the constraint property typed by the constraint block.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Exporting an instance as a slot value of the constraint property.

402984463

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Evaluating expressions' space='CST2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mathematical equation' space='CST2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b499149-7d9e-4c32-a902-b91152c31ca9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402984464</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a523c23c-1cd5-4ddf-8b2b-fc889766c906"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402984466</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="491e0a58-cf88-478e-81bb-67182c8b8264"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402984465</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A logical expression is an expression that uses comparison operators. You can use a logical expression to define a constraint. Cameo Simulation Toolkit uses the expression to validate values at runtime.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee9f0260-2353-4e52-b390-032a8411a006"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A logical expression must contain a comparison operator (==, &lt;, &gt;, &lt;=, &gt;=, and &lt;&gt;) as shown in the following examples:</p><ul><li><em>val1 &gt;= &quot;500&quot;</em> is a valid expression.<em><br /></em></li><li><em>val2.equals(&quot;500&quot;) == true</em> is a valid expression.</li><li><em>val2.equals(&quot;500&quot;)</em> is an invalid expression.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Red background for the labels and text fields in UI</h2><p>If some constraints failed during evaluation, Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) will highlight the values in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane with a red background to denote that the values fail the constraint. All parent nodes (Name column) that contain the failed constraints will have a red background (until the root one in the tree view), which indicates failures deeper in the structure tree. You can also see these values in the <strong>Simulation Console</strong> pane. </p><p class="Standard">Furthermore, both the values and validation that are highlighted must always be consistent with the labels and text boxes of both the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="UI modeling diagram simulation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UI modeling diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and the HTML UI during simulation.</p><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="redbackground.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Logical expression" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Red backgrounds indicating failures during evaluation.</h6><p class="Standard"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="460efdba-3bcb-43ba-9399-06b4ef36d21b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Neither green background nor tooltip will be used for &quot;passed&quot; verification status for both Swing mockups and HTML UI.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard">A tooltip will also appear if you hover the mouse over the failed constraint. Each parent of the failed constraint will appear with a contextual path &quot;dot notation&quot; starting from the node on which tooltip is shown. </p><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="logicalExpression2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Logical expression" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">The parent node with a red background indicates failures deeper in the structure tree.</h6><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="logicalExpression3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Logical expression" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Nodes with green background indicating valid values.</h6><p>This example uses the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="equationSysml.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Logical expression" /></ri:attachment><ac:link-body>block <strong>Circle</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> previously shown in the section <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Mathematical equation" /></ac:link>. If we modify the expression of the constraint block <strong>Circle</strong> <strong>Area</strong> by changing the assignment operator &quot;<strong>=</strong>&quot; to the equality operator &quot;<strong>==</strong>&quot;, the parametric evaluator will use the expression to validate the values of both area and radius. If the result is false, the values will be highlighted in red (see Figure: The parent node with a red background indicates failures deeper in the structure tree). But, if the result is true, the values will be highlighted in green (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cc81ea82-e052-46f3-9aaf-f96d747383e1">see Figure: Nodes with green background indicating valid values</ac:inline-comment-marker>). </p><h2>Finding a constraint object that binds the value property</h2><p>You can find the constraint object to which the value of the property is bound in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><p>To find a constraint object to which a value property is bound</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Right-click a constraint object row in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane, and select <strong>Go</strong> <strong>To</strong>. The constraint to which the value of the property is bound will appear, as shown in the figure below.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="logicalExpression4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Logical expression" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Finding a constraint object that binds the value property through the shortcut menu. </h6></li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />When you export the object to the instance specification, if the exported object contains a constraint object (an object of a constraint block) and if the expression of the constraint block is a boolean expression, Cameo Simulation Toolkit will set the result of the evaluation, which is a literal boolean, to a slot defined by the constraint property typed by the constraint block. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="logicalExpression5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Logical expression" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" />Exporting an instance as a slot value of the constraint property.</h6><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8570066-61e1-4e77-8ffc-27dedf29d275"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402984463</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Evaluating expressions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024x" ri:content-title="Mathematical equation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
